translation: The revelation of the Qur'an is from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise
commentary:
تَنزِيلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ

The revelation of the Book is from Allah ﷻ, the Almighty, the All-Wise.

Surah Az-Zumar begins a series of stories that illustrate the practical side of Tawheed - not just believing in the oneness of Allah ﷻ, but understanding what that means in terms of consequences and impact in our daily lives.

The Surah opens by establishing the divine origin of the Quran with two of Allah's names: Al-Aziz (العزيز) - the Ultimate Authority, and Al-Hakim (الحكيم) - the All-Wise. Just as the previous Surah emphasized that the heavens and earth weren't created without purpose, this ayah establishes that you've been given a book with purpose and reason.

translation: Indeed, We have sent down to you the Book, [O Muhammad], in truth. So worship Allah, [being] sincere to Him in religion
commentary:
إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ فَٱعْبُدِ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ

We have certainly revealed to you the Book in truth, so worship Allah, being sincere to Him in religion.

This ayah introduces a fundamental concept that will be repeated throughout the Surah: sincerity. The word مُخْلِصًا means to make something exclusive and pure. The root خ-ل-ص carries the meaning of emptying out all impurities so that only the pure thing itself remains.

When Allah ﷻ says to worship Him مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ, He's commanding that our entire lifestyle be made sincerely and exclusively for Him. The word دين is often translated as "religion," but it's more accurately understood as "lifestyle" - encompassing all decisions and choices in life. The word deen literally means "judgment," which is why we call Judgment Day يوم الدين.

Understanding worship requires recognizing that there's both a body and a soul to slavery to Allah ﷻ. Just as the human body has a physical form with a soul inside, worship has an outward form and an inner reality. Prayer, fasting, Hajj, dress code, and dietary laws - these are the body of worship, what worship looks like externally. But there's also the soul of worship, and that soul is an attitude that permeates everything: business dealings, academic pursuits, career goals, life plans, family decisions, marriage choices. This attitude of servitude must invade every aspect of life.

It's possible for someone to have the body of worship without the soul. Someone might be dedicated to prayer and fasting, follow all the dietary restrictions, but still live as a materialist with aspirations limited entirely to this worldly life. When the body is present but the soul is absent, that's when we see the disconnect the Quran addresses repeatedly. These stories in Az-Zumar will examine the soul of slavery - the genuine, sincere inside of servitude to Allah ﷻ.

translation: Unquestionably, for Allah is the pure religion. And those who take protectors besides Him [say], "We only worship them that they may bring us nearer to Allah in position." Indeed, Allah will judge between them concerning that over which they differ. Indeed, Allah does not guide he who is a liar and [confirmed] disbeliever
commentary:
أَلَا لِلَّهِ ٱلدِّينُ ٱلْخَالِصُ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ مَا نَعْبُدُهُمْ إِلَّا لِيُقَرِّبُونَآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ زُلْفَىٰٓ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ فِى مَا هُمْ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى مَنْ هُوَ كَـٰذِبٌ كَفَّارٌ

Unquestionably, for Allah is the pure religion. And those who take protectors besides Him [say], "We only worship them that they may bring us nearer to Allah in position." Indeed, Allah will judge between them concerning that over which they differ. Indeed, Allah does not guide he who is a liar and [confirmed] disbeliever.

أَلَا لِلَّهِ ٱلدِّينُ ٱلْخَالِصُ - Listen carefully: to Allah ﷻ alone belongs the pure lifestyle. This means Allah ﷻ only accepts complete devotion. He doesn't want partial commitment from you, doesn't want a half-hearted effort, doesn't want you to just go through the motions to get it over with. Sometimes our prayer becomes a disgusting excuse for salah - that's not what Allah ﷻ wants. He wants الدين الخالص - the pure, unadulterated lifestyle of submission.

The ayah then addresses those who take protective guardians and authorities other than Allah ﷻ. They rationalize their practice by saying, "We don't worship them except to help us get closer to Allah in status and rank" - seeking زُلْفَى, nearness and elevated position. But the previous Surah already taught us what truly brings you closest to Allah ﷻ, what gives you that spiritual proximity.

Allah ﷻ will judge between people in all their disagreements. And certainly, He never guides - He refuses to guide - the one who is كَـٰذِبٌ كَفَّارٌ : a liar and excessively ungrateful. These two qualities go hand in hand when someone claims to serve Allah ﷻ while serving other authorities and desires.

translation: If Allah had intended to take a son, He could have chosen from what He creates whatever He willed. Exalted is He; He is Allah, the One, the Prevailing
commentary:
لَّوْ أَرَادَ ٱللَّهُ أَن يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا لَّٱصْطَفَىٰ مِمَّا يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ ۖ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْوَٰحِدُ ٱلْقَهَّارُ

If Allah had intended to take a son, He could have chosen from what He creates whatever He willed. Exalted is He; He is Allah, the One, the Prevailing.

If Allah ﷻ had wanted to take a son - hypothetically speaking - He could have chosen whatever He wanted from His creation. But سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ - He is far above and beyond such allegations. Why would He take any creation as a child when He is Allah ﷻ, الْوَٰحِدُ  - the One, and الْقَهَّارُ - the Dominant, the Irresistible, the One whose authority cannot be denied?

translation: He created the heavens and earth in truth. He wraps the night over the day and wraps the day over the night and has subjected the sun and the moon, each running [its course] for a specified term. Unquestionably, He is the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver
commentary:
خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ يُكَوِّرُ ٱلَّيْلَ عَلَى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُكَوِّرُ ٱلنَّهَارَ عَلَى ٱلَّيْلِ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ ۖ كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۗ أَلَا هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْغَفَّـٰرُ

He created the heavens and earth in truth. He wraps the night over the day and wraps the day over the night and has subjected the sun and the moon, each running [its course] for a specified term. Unquestionably, He is the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver.

He created the heavens and earth بِٱلْحَقِّ  - with purpose, with truth. The verb يُكَوِّرُ describes how He makes the night wrap around and fold over the day, and makes the day fold over the night. He has subdued and domesticated the sun and moon - كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى- all of them flowing, running their course until a deadline that is well-named and appointed.

You had better know: هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْغَفَّـٰرُ  - He is the Ultimate Authority and the Extremely Forgiving. These two names together carry a powerful implication: you better recognize Allah's authority because Judgment Day is coming, and the deadline for the sun and moon is approaching. At the same time, you better take advantage of the fact that Allah ﷻ continually, repeatedly offers the opportunity for forgiveness - He is الْغَفَّـٰر . This opportunity won't be available forever.

translation: He created you from one soul. Then He made from it its mate, and He produced for you from the grazing livestock eight mates. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation, within three darknesses. That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs dominion. There is no deity except Him, so how are you averted
commentary:
خَلَقَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَٰحِدَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْهَا زَوْجَهَا وَأَنزَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ ثَمَـٰنِيَةَ أَزْوَٰجٍ ۚ يَخْلُقُكُمْ فِى بُطُونِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ خَلْقًا مِّنۢ بَعْدِ خَلْقٍ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتٍ ثَلَـٰثٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُصْرَفُونَ

He created you from one soul. Then He made from it its mate, and He produced for you from the grazing livestock eight mates. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation, within three darknesses. That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs dominion. There is no deity except Him, so how are you deluded?

This ayah reviews the conclusion of the previous Surah - the story of Adam and Hawwa (AS) - captured in a single phrase: He created you from نَّفْسٍ وَٰحِدَةٍ  - a single entity, a single soul. Then He made from it زَوْجَهَا  - its spouse. Adam (AS) was created first, and Hawwa (AS) was made from him.

There's something remarkable here when we consider modern biology. The contemporary scientific argument is that life began with single-cell creatures that didn't have two genders - they were one - and then cells started splitting and eventually genders emerged. When Allah ﷻ describes the creation of human beings, He describes one gender first - Adam (AS) - and from him the female is extracted. It begins as one, and then the female comes forth. Allah knows best the deeper implications of this parallel.

The ayah mentions the eight kinds of pairs of cattle, which was detailed in previous surahs. Then it describes the miracle of human development: يَخْلُقُكُمْ فِى بُطُونِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ خَلْقًا مِّنۢ بَعْدِ خَلْقٍ (He creates you in the bellies of your mothers, creation after another creation). You begin looking like a tiny dot, then like a piece of clinging flesh, then gradually form into a recognizable shape - you become a completely different creature at each stage. If you look at an ultrasound after two weeks, then after three months, then after six months, you see an entirely different being each time.

This development happens فِى ظُلُمَـٰتٍ ثَلَـٰثٍ - within three darknesses: the darkness of the belly of the mother, the darkness of the uterus within it, and the darkness of the amniotic sac where the child hangs - one layer of darkness after another.

After describing these profound signs of creation, Allah ﷻ declares: ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ (That is Allah, your Lord). لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ (To Him belongs all dominion, all kingdom). لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ (There is no one except Him to be worshiped and obeyed). فَأَنَّىٰ تُصْرَفُونَ (So how are you turned away?) - How are you being diverted in other directions? The imagery is powerful: Shaytan calls you this way, that way, another way - how are you turning away from the obvious truth?

translation: If you disbelieve - indeed, Allah is Free from need of you. And He does not approve for His servants disbelief. And if you are grateful, He approves it for you; and no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you about what you used to do. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts
commentary:
إِن تَكْفُرُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ عَنكُمْ ۖ وَلَا يَرْضَىٰ لِعِبَادِهِ ٱلْكُفْرَ ۖ وَإِن تَشْكُرُوا۟ يَرْضَهُ لَكُمْ ۗ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۗ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم مَّرْجِعُكُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ

If you disbelieve - indeed, Allah is Free from need of you. And He does not approve for His servants disbelief. And if you are grateful, He approves it for you. And no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you about what you used to do. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

If you choose to disbelieve, if you choose ingratitude, then فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ عَنكُمْ (Allah is completely free of need from you). He doesn't need you. However - and this is crucial - وَلَا يَرْضَىٰ لِعِبَادِهِ ٱلْكُفْرَ (He is not pleased with disbelief for His slaves). He doesn't want that for them, even though He doesn't need them. وَإِن تَشْكُرُوا۟ يَرْضَهُ لَكُمْ (And if you are grateful, He will be pleased with it for you).

وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ (No bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another) - nobody will carry someone else's load on Judgment Day. ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم مَّرْجِعُكُمْ (Then to your Master you will all be taken back), فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (and He will inform you very thoroughly about what you used to do).

إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (Indeed, He is Knowing of what is within the chests) - He truly knows the nature of what lies in the heart. The mention of الصُّدُور (the chests, the hearts) at the end of this conversation is significant, because that's where sincerity resides. The entire discussion about خلاص (sincerity) points back to this: what's in your chest, your heart, your inner reality.

translation: And when adversity touches man, he calls upon his Lord, turning to Him [alone]; then when He bestows on him a favor from Himself, he forgets Him whom he called upon before, and he attributes to Allah equals to mislead [people] from His way. Say, "Enjoy your disbelief for a little; indeed, you are of the companions of the Fire
commentary:
وَإِذَا مَسَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ ضُرٌّ دَعَا رَبَّهُۥ مُنِيبًا إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ إِذَا خَوَّلَهُۥ نِعْمَةً مِّنْهُ نَسِىَ مَا كَانَ يَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ مِن قَبْلُ وَجَعَلَ لِلَّهِ أَندَادًا لِّيُضِلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ ۚ قُلْ تَمَتَّعْ بِكُفْرِكَ قَلِيلًا ۖ إِنَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلنَّارِ

And when adversity touches man, he calls upon his Lord, turning to Him [alone]; then when He bestows on him a favor from Himself, he forgets Him whom he called upon before, and he attributes to Allah equals to mislead [people] from His way. Say, "Enjoy your disbelief for a little. Indeed, you are of the companions of the Fire."

When harm touches the human being, he calls upon his Master, مُنِيبًا إِلَيْهِ - turning himself back spiritually and completely to Him. Then - ثُمَّ إِذَا خَوَّلَهُۥ نِعْمَةً مِّنْهُ (when He wraps him up in a favor from Himself) - there's beautiful imagery here. The word خَوَّلَ means to wrap up, like gift wrapping.

This wrapping imagery appears throughout the Quran in profound ways. Our soul (ruh) is wrapped up by the body. That body is wrapped up by the gift of clothes. Those clothes are sheltered by the gift of our homes. Those homes are wrapped by our neighborhoods. Those neighborhoods wrapped by cities. Those cities wrapped by countries. Those countries wrapped by this planet. That planet wrapped by the sky, then the second sky, then the third - we're wrapped up and folded, one layer after another. What happens on Judgment Day? All these layers are peeled away, and your essence has to stand directly before Allah ﷻ.

When someone is wrapped up in favors - covered like someone wrapped in a blanket - they become blinded by the comfort around them. They can't see clearly anymore. So when this person finds himself wrapped in luxury and blessings, نَسِىَ مَا كَانَ يَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ مِن قَبْلُ (he forgets what he used to call upon before). He forgets the distress that made him turn to Allah ﷻ.

Worse still: وَجَعَلَ لِلَّهِ أَندَادًا (he sets up competitors, equals for Allah) لِّيُضِلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ (so that he misleads others from His path). He doesn't just set up partners with Allah ﷻ for himself - he starts misguiding others too. This happens because people want to be like those who have wealth and comfort. People want homes like their homes, lifestyles like their lifestyles. So if this blessed person makes his money through haram means, others say, "Well, he did it, we can do it too." He sets a terrible precedent and starts pulling others away from Allah's path.

قُلْ تَمَتَّعْ بِكُفْرِكَ قَلِيلًا (Tell him: Enjoy your disbelief for a little while). إِنَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلنَّارِ (You are from the people of the Fire).

translation: Is one who is devoutly obedient during periods of the night, prostrating and standing [in prayer], fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord, [like one who does not]? Say, "Are those who know equal to those who do not know?" Only they will remember [who are] people of understanding
commentary:
أَمَّنْ هُوَ قَـٰنِتٌ ءَانَآءَ ٱلَّيْلِ سَاجِدًا وَقَآئِمًا يَحْذَرُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ وَيَرْجُوا۟ رَحْمَةَ رَبِّهِۦ ۗ قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِى ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ ۗ إِنَّمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ

Is one who is devoutly obedient during periods of the night, prostrating and standing [in prayer], fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord, [like one who does not]? Say, "Are those who know equal to those who do not know?" Only they will remember [who are] people of understanding.

This is one of the remarkable stylistic features of this Surah. Allah ﷻ asks a question but doesn't complete the sentence - He leaves you to fill in the obvious conclusion. It's like asking: "What do you think should happen to someone who prays, fasts, and is sincere to Allah?" You start the question, "As for that kind of person..." but don't finish it. The answer is obvious: they should be in Jannah, they should be rewarded, they should get what they deserve. But Allah ﷻ doesn't state it - you should be able to complete it yourself.

أَمَّنْ هُوَ قَـٰنِتٌ (As for the one who is qanit) - ready and willing to obey, enthusiastic about obedience. ءَانَآءَ ٱلَّيْلِ (During the hours of the night), سَاجِدًا وَقَآئِمًا (in prostration and standing before Allah). يَحْذَرُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ (Cautious and fearful about the Hereafter). وَيَرْجُوا۟ رَحْمَةَ رَبِّهِۦ (Full of hope for the mercy of his Master) - balancing fear and hope perfectly.

Then comes the directive: قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِى ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (Say: Are those who know equal to those who don't know?). People often quote this part of the ayah when discussing the benefits of seeking Islamic knowledge - studying tafsir, fiqh, aqeedah. That application is valid, but within the context of the ayah itself, what is Allah ﷻ specifically talking about?

He's talking about someone who knows about the Hereafter. Can the person who knows what's coming and is worried about it be the same as the person who doesn't know? Someone who knows there's a flood coming versus someone who has no idea - are they going to act the same? Of course not.

إِنَّمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (Only people of sound minds, people of sharp intellect, will take benefit from reminders). People with functioning intellects look at the signs and figure out what's coming. They see too many clouds gathering and think, "I better check the news. Let me turn to the weather station." That's sharp awareness. You look at the signs and you take heed. Allah ﷻ is saying that people who know and people who don't know - they're not going to be the same in their response.

translation: Say, "O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world is good, and the earth of Allah is spacious. Indeed, the patient will be given their reward without account
commentary:
قُلْ يَـٰعِبَادِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ ۗ وَأَرْضُ ٱللَّهِ وَٰسِعَةٌ ۗ إِنَّمَا يُوَفَّى ٱلصَّـٰبِرُونَ أَجْرَهُم بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ

Say, "O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world is good, and the earth of Allah is spacious. Indeed, the patient will be given their reward without account."

يَـٰعِبَادِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ (O My slaves who have believed) - notice the beautiful, intimate address: "My slaves." ٱتَّقُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ (Be cautious of your Master, have taqwa of your Lord).

لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ (For those who excel in this world, who do things beautifully in this world, there will be beautiful things, good rewards).

وَأَرْضُ ٱللَّهِ وَٰسِعَةٌ (And the earth of Allah is vast, spacious). This is a profound statement for those facing persecution or difficulty practicing their faith. If you can't practice your religion freely where you are, if you're facing oppression, then go somewhere else. Allah's earth is vast. This is a reference to hijrah - migration for the sake of Allah ﷻ.

إِنَّمَا يُوَفَّى ٱلصَّـٰبِرُونَ أَجْرَهُم بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (The people of sabr, the patient ones, will be given their reward without any calculation, without any restrictions). Who are these patient people? Those who travel and migrate for the sake of Allah ﷻ so they can worship Him better. Those who leave oppression, who say, "We can't say لا إله إلا الله freely here," and they go somewhere they can.

Leaving your family, leaving your home, leaving the place where you spent your childhood - this is not easy. It requires immense patience. And Allah ﷻ says those who show this kind of sabr for His sake will receive provisions and rewards beyond any measurement or restriction.

translation: Say, [O Muhammad], "Indeed, I have been commanded to worship Allah, [being] sincere to Him in religion
commentary:
قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ

Say, [O Muhammad], "Indeed, I have been commanded to worship Allah, [being] sincere to Him in religion."

قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أُمِرْتُ (Say: I have been commanded) - the Prophet ﷺ is instructed to announce this clearly. أَنْ أَعْبُدَ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ (To enslave myself to Allah, sincerely making my entire lifestyle for Him).

This teaches us something essential: the message of the Prophet ﷺ wasn't simply about believing in one God. There were others in Makkah who believed in one God - that wasn't the controversial part. But this belief couldn't be restricted to acts of ritual worship alone. The Prophet ﷺ was saying that this belief should affect how you do business, how you treat baby girls, how you treat orphans, how you measure and weigh commodities at the marketplace. This is when the lifestyle becomes sincere to Allah ﷻ through and through.

When you properly understand Tawheed, it forces a code of ethics into everything you do. There's an important distinction to understand here: law and ethics are two different things. Law speaks to what's permissible and prohibited, to punishments and regulations. According to Islamic law, Prophet Ayyub (AS) had to fulfill his oath and strike his wife, but he did so with a bundle of soft grass. That's law - a technical fulfillment of an oath.

But then there are matters of ethics: principles of justice, fairness, equality, not being biased, not being racist, not favoring your family over others in matters of justice and inheritance. For these ethical matters, you don't necessarily need a detailed fatwa or specific verse from the Quran. Fairness and justice are embedded inside the human being. Your lifestyle just needs one thing: awareness that Allah ﷻ is watching. That's all you need to be set on the right track.

When Muslims become corrupt, they often try to use the law of Allah ﷻ to circumvent universal ethics. They seek fatwas to justify not giving their daughter her proper inheritance, or to find exceptions to make money through questionable means. They try to use the law of Allah ﷻ to avoid the justice for which that law was revealed in the first place. The Shariah is like icing on the cake - but the cake itself is justice. The law of Allah ﷻ crowns and perfects the foundation of justice.

Unfortunately, we sometimes see ultra-religious communities where the law is emphasized intensely - halal and haram are discussed constantly, people are learning fiqh and memorizing Quran - yet justice is barely a concern. The mosques are full, people are getting ijazahs in hadith and fiqh, but it remains a class-based society with deep inequalities. People can discuss the minutiae of wudu and ritual purity, but when it comes to treating their servants, treating foreigners, or treating people from lower economic classes, the ethics completely disappear.

The commitment to Shariah appears to be there, but the ethics have been entirely circumvented. This ayah reminds us that مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ means your entire lifestyle - not just your ritual practice, but your ethics, your character, your treatment of others, your business dealings, everything - must be sincerely and exclusively for Allah ﷻ.

translation: And I have been commanded to be the first [among you] of the Muslims
commentary:
وَأُمِرْتُ لِأَنْ أَكُونَ أَوَّلَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ

The Prophet ﷺ declares that he has been commanded to be the first of those who submit themselves to Allah ﷻ. This isn't merely a chronological statement but one of priority and excellence in submission. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ carries the weight of being the foremost example of complete surrender to the Divine Will.

translation: Say, "Indeed I fear, if I should disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day
commentary:
قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّى عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ

The Prophet ﷺ is instructed to say, "I am in fact afraid - if I disobey my Master, of the punishment of a tremendous day." This ayah reveals something powerful about prophetic consciousness. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ tells the disbelievers that threats from them don't move him. What moves him is the fear of disappointing Allah ﷻ. That's real fear - not fear of people's reactions or worldly consequences, but fear of failing to fulfill the most important mission ever given to any human being.

translation: Say, "Allah [alone] do I worship, sincere to Him in my religion
commentary:
قُلِ ٱللَّهَ أَعْبُدُ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُۥ دِينِى

Once again, the emphasis returns: "Allah alone is the One I enslave myself to, making my religion sincere, purely sincere, only to Him." The repetition of إِخْلَاص (sincerity) and دِين (religion, way of life) throughout these passages isn't redundant. Your mind can understand something after hearing it once, but your heart requires repeated exposure. Every time Allah ﷻ sends this message, He breaks through another barrier, then another, then another, until it penetrates completely.

translation: So worship what you will besides Him." Say, "Indeed, the losers are the ones who will lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Unquestionably, that is the manifest loss
commentary:
فَٱعْبُدُوا۟ مَا شِئْتُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَهْلِيهِمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ أَلَا ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْخُسْرَانُ ٱلْمُبِينُ

"Then keep worshiping whatever you worship besides Him." This statement carries the weight of ultimatum. The discourse addresses both seen and unseen realities throughout. There are physical idols that people bow to, but there are also unseen idols that enslave us just as effectively. Your greed is an unseen idol. Your temptations are unseen idols. Your addictions are unseen idols. These internal false gods must be shattered just as surely as any physical statue.

Allah ﷻ then defines ultimate loss: "The real losers are those who have lost themselves and their families on the Day of Standing." You should know with certainty that this represents the clear, manifest loss. Loss isn't just about failing to gain something - it's about destroying what you already had. They lose their own souls and the opportunity to be with their families in eternal bliss.

translation: They will have canopies of fire above them and below them, canopies. By that Allah threatens His servants. O My servants, then fear Me
commentary:
لَهُم مِّن فَوْقِهِمْ ظُلَلٌ مِّنَ ٱلنَّارِ وَمِن تَحْتِهِمْ ظُلَلٌ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يُخَوِّفُ ٱللَّهُ بِهِۦ عِبَادَهُۥ ۚ يَـٰعِبَادِ فَٱتَّقُونِ

For these losers, there will be canopies of fire above them and canopies of fire beneath them. Fire typically burns bright, but the Fire of Hell produces black flames - fire with shadows. The imagery is suffocating: layers upon layers of darkness and heat, above and below, with no escape.

Allah ﷻ states explicitly: "With this, Allah instills fear into His slaves." Then comes the direct address: "O My slaves, then be afraid. Protect yourselves." Notice the terminology shift. The word خَوْف is translated as fear, while تَقْوَىٰ is also often translated as fear. But when two related words appear together in Arabic, they carry distinct meanings. Allah ﷻ says He's instilling khawf so that you develop taqwa. Khawf is the emotional response of fear, while taqwa is the conscious protection and precaution you take as a result of that fear. They work together but serve different purposes.

translation: But those who have avoided Taghut, lest they worship it, and turned back to Allah - for them are good tidings. So give good tidings to My servants
commentary:
وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱجْتَنَبُوا۟ ٱلطَّـٰغُوتَ أَن يَعْبُدُوهَا وَأَنَابُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ لَهُمُ ٱلْبُشْرَىٰ ۚ فَبَشِّرْ عِبَادِ

Now the scene shifts dramatically. Those who were able to avoid الطَّاغُوت and falling into slavery to it, and who turned back to Allah ﷻ - for them belongs the good news exclusively.

What is taghut? The word comes from the root meaning rebellion. Taghut must meet specific conditions: it's a living entity that communicates with people and has authority over them, pulling them toward rebellion against Allah ﷻ. There are seen forms of taghut - tyrannical rulers like Pharaoh who tell people that obeying Allah ﷻ will get them arrested, that wearing hijab will cost them their jobs. These authorities command disobedience to the Divine.

But there's also unseen taghut - your own greed, your lust, your laziness. These internal forces have authority over you, pulling you to disobey Allah ﷻ. When you successfully avoid both forms of taghut and turn back to Allah ﷻ, you own the real congratulations. "Then congratulate My slaves."

translation: Who listen to speech and follow the best of it. Those are the ones Allah has guided, and those are people of understanding
commentary:
ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَمِعُونَ ٱلْقَوْلَ فَيَتَّبِعُونَ أَحْسَنَهُۥٓ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ هَدَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمْ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ

Who are these slaves worthy of congratulations? Those who listen to speech and follow the best of it. The word الْقَوْلَ can refer to any speech, but many scholars say it refers specifically to the Quran itself.

On one level, this describes people who hear various statements and focus on what's beneficial. They don't obsess over negative comments or dwell on criticisms. When they hear something, they extract the good and leave the bad. We often do the opposite - someone tells us something positive and we barely register it, but mention something negative and we're all ears. These believers actively seek the good in what they hear.

On another level, أَحْسَنَهُ - "the best of it" - can mean they follow the Word of Allah ﷻ in the best possible way. They don't just hear and acknowledge; they implement with excellence, striving to follow Divine guidance to the utmost of their ability.

These are the people Allah ﷻ has truly guided. These are the people of sound intellect - أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ , possessors of pure, functioning minds.

translation: Then, is one who has deserved the decree of punishment [to be guided]? Then, can you save one who is in the Fire
commentary:
أَفَمَنْ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ كَلِمَةُ ٱلْعَذَابِ أَفَأَنتَ تُنقِذُ مَن فِى ٱلنَّارِ

Then comes a rhetorical question to the Prophet ﷺ: "Is the one upon whom the word of punishment has become justified...?" The question starts but doesn't complete immediately, building tension. Then: "Can you rescue someone who is in the Fire?"

When the verdict of punishment has already been deserved, when it's rightful and the sentence has passed, you cannot save that person, O Messenger. Your job is to remind and warn, not to force salvation upon those who have sealed their fate.

translation: But those who have feared their Lord - for them are chambers, above them chambers built high, beneath which rivers flow. [This is] the promise of Allah. Allah does not fail in [His] promise
commentary:
لَـٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ رَبَّهُمْ لَهُمْ غُرَفٌ مِّن فَوْقِهَا غُرَفٌ مَّبْنِيَّةٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ لَا يُخْلِفُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمِيعَادَ

But those who were cautious and protective of their relationship with their Master - those who feared disappointing Him - for them there are lofty chambers. The word غُرَف suggests spacious, elevated rooms. And above those rooms? More rooms. Well-built structures extending upward like magnificent castles, and at their base, rivers flowing beneath.

This is the promise of Allah ﷻ. And Allah does not break His promise. When He gives His word, it is absolute and unshakeable.

translation: Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky and makes it flow as springs [and rivers] in the earth; then He produces thereby crops of varying colors; then they dry and you see them turned yellow; then He makes them [scattered] debris. Indeed in that is a reminder for those of understanding
commentary:
أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۭ فَسَلَكَهُۥ يَنَـٰبِيعَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ يُخْرِجُ بِهِۦ زَرْعًۭا مُّخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ ثُمَّ يَهِيجُ فَتَرَىٰهُ مُصْفَرًّۭا ثُمَّ يَجْعَلُهُۥ حُطَـٰمًا ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَذِكْرَىٰ لِأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ

Haven't you seen how Allah ﷻ sends down water from the sky? Then He causes it to penetrate the earth, forming springs and fountains. From this water, He brings forth crops of different colors and textures. The plants mature and flourish. But then, if you keep watching, you'll see them turn yellow. Eventually they become dry, brittle, worthless - just crushed matter you step on without thought.

In all of this, there is an ultimate reminder for people of pure understanding.

The connection to the previous ayah is striking. Allah ﷻ described gardens in Paradise with rivers flowing through them. Then He draws our attention to gardens in this world - they also have water, they also have beautiful plants. But notice the difference: the worldly garden doesn't last. The flower blooms beautifully, but the season passes. It yellows, withers, dies, becomes worthless. If you want a garden that remains perpetually fresh and alive, that's Jannah. The gardens of this world cannot endure - they're temporary by design, reminding us not to become attached to what inevitably fades.

translation: So is one whose breast Allah has expanded to [accept] Islam and he is upon a light from his Lord [like one whose heart rejects it]? Then woe to those whose hearts are hardened against the remembrance of Allah. Those are in manifest error
commentary:
أَفَمَن شَرَحَ ٱللَّهُ صَدْرَهُۥ لِلْإِسْلَـٰمِ فَهُوَ عَلَىٰ نُورٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۚ فَوَيْلٌ لِّلْقَـٰسِيَةِ قُلُوبُهُم مِّن ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ

What about the one whose chest Allah ﷻ has expanded for Islam? The verb شَرَحَ means to open up, to expand, to make spacious. The word صَدْر refers to the chest, the place where the heart resides and where emotions settle.

When something makes you comfortable, when you feel at home, that's the state of the chest being at ease. Some company makes you uncomfortable, some places disturb you, some news unsettles you. But then there are spaces where you feel completely at peace, completely at home. That's what this ayah describes - when Allah ﷻ expands your chest for Islam, everything about submission to Him brings you peace and comfort.

For such a person, salah relaxes them. Eating halal brings them contentment. Conversely, the thought of missing salah disturbs them deeply. The air feels tight, they can't breathe properly. If you're stuck in traffic and realize Maghrib is slipping away, anxiety builds inside you. This was the reality of Prophet Sulaiman (AS) in the previous surah when he missed his prayer - he was profoundly bothered because his chest only expands in submission to Allah ﷻ. When he realizes he's done something outside that submission, there's internal disturbance.

"He is then committed to a light from his Master." Where is this light? In the chest, in the heart. The revelation itself is light, and it meets the light that Allah ﷻ places inside the believer.

But then comes the contrast: "Woe to those hard of heart from the remembrance of Allah ﷻ." Their hearts have become hard as a result of not remembering Allah ﷻ. The chest houses the heart, and when the heart hardens, it's like glass covered in solidified dirt. No light can penetrate. The revelation remains outside, unable to enter. These people are in clear, obvious misguidance.

translation: Allah has sent down the best statement: a consistent Book wherein is reiteration. The skins shiver therefrom of those who fear their Lord; then their skins and their hearts relax at the remembrance of Allah. That is the guidance of Allah by which He guides whom He wills. And one whom Allah leaves astray - for him there is no guide
commentary:
ٱللَّهُ نَزَّلَ أَحْسَنَ ٱلْحَدِيثِ كِتَـٰبًا مُّتَشَـٰبِهًا مَّثَانِىَ تَقْشَعِرُّ مِنْهُ جُلُودُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ ثُمَّ تَلِينُ جُلُودُهُمْ وَقُلُوبُهُمْ إِلَىٰ ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُدَى ٱللَّهِ يَهْدِى بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍ

Allah ﷻ is the One who sent down, little by little, the best of all speech - أَحْسَنَ الْحَدِيثِ. This Book is مُتَشَابِهًا- its parts are consistent with each other, some portions resembling others in style and message. It is مَثَانِيَ- filled with opportunities to praise Allah ﷻ, replete with verses of remembrance and glorification.

When people who truly fear their Master hear this Quran, the hair on their skin rises. تَقْشَعِرُّ describes that physical sensation of goosebumps, the hair standing on end. This isn't about the reciter's voice - it's not the qari causing this reaction. It's the words themselves, the realization that hits you: This is Allah ﷻ speaking to me.

We all believe the Quran is Allah's Word, but when that reality moves from intellectual knowledge (here, in the mind) to experiential understanding (here, in the heart), it shakes you. You feel an overwhelming presence. It's like the nervous, awestruck feeling when you encounter someone of immense authority. Sometimes in a moment during prayer or recitation, the reality hits you that you're truly in the presence of the Most High, and your body responds involuntarily.

Then their skins soften. The tension releases, the muscles relax. Their hearts turn back to the remembrance of Allah ﷻ. Why? Because the Quran contains both fear and hope. One moment you hear verses that make you tremble with fear of Allah's punishment, your skin tight with anxiety. Then you hear verses about His mercy, His forgiveness, His love for those who turn to Him, and your heart melts, your body relaxes.

"That is the guidance of Allah ﷻ" - meaning this is how you experience His guidance. This physical and spiritual response is part of being guided. He guides with this whoever He wills. And whoever Allah ﷻ misguides, you will never find anyone to guide them.

translation: Then is he who will shield with his face the worst of the punishment on the Day of Resurrection [like one secure from it]? And it will be said to the wrongdoers, "Taste what you used to earn
commentary:
أَفَمَن يَتَّقِى بِوَجْهِهِۦ سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ وَقِيلَ لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ ذُوقُوا۟ مَا كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ

What about the one who tries to protect himself by means of his own face from the worst of punishment on the Day of Resurrection? 

The image is devastating. Normally, when you protect yourself from danger, you use your hands. You shield yourself, you push away the threat. But this person's hands are bound. The only defense he has against the punishment is his face - the very part of the body you most want to protect. He has to use his face as a shield because nothing else is available.

And the wrongdoers are told: "Taste what you used to earn." The consequences of their choices finally come due.

translation: Those before them denied, and punishment came upon them from where they did not perceive
commentary:
كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَأَتَىٰهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ

Those who came before them also denied the truth. Then the punishment came to them from where they couldn't even perceive it, from a direction they never anticipated.

translation: So Allah made them taste disgrace in worldly life. But the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew
commentary:
فَأَذَاقَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْخِزْىَ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ

Allah ﷻ made them taste humiliation in worldly life. And certainly, the punishment of the Hereafter is far greater - if only they knew.

Consider the great kingdom of Pharaoh, what everyone recognized as the mighty Egyptian empire. One source of their power was the Nile River. Control over the river meant control over agriculture, which meant economic dominance, which funded a massive military. Their strength flowed from that river. Yet the very thing they drew their power from became the instrument of their destruction. The waters that gave them life swallowed them in death. Their source of pride became their ultimate humiliation.

If only they had known.

translation: And We have certainly presented for the people in this Qur'an from every [kind of] example - that they might remember
commentary:
وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِى هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ

We have certainly struck for humanity in this Quran every kind of example, all varieties of illustrations and parables. Why? لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ - so that perhaps they will remember, they will take heed, they will benefit from this reminder.

translation: [It is] an Arabic Qur'an, without any deviance that they might become righteous
commentary:
قُرْءَانًا عَرَبِيًّا غَيْرَ ذِى عِوَجٍ لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ

An Arabic Quran without any عِوَج- without crookedness, without deviation, without twists and turns. It's straightforward. It's not complicated. It's not abstract or deliberately obscure.

This directness is actually remarkable when you consider other philosophical and religious traditions. Study a philosophy text and see how much you understand on first reading. You'll find yourself going in circles, lost in abstractions. Talk to followers of various Eastern philosophies about their beliefs - you'll get vague responses about everything being "connected" and "peaceful," but nothing concrete or clear.

When Greek philosophers first encountered the Quran, do you know what they criticized? They said it was too straightforward. Not twisted enough. It doesn't take you on intellectual loops until you're thoroughly confused. There's no entertainment in that kind of clarity, they complained.

But that's precisely the point. غَيْرَ ذِى عِوَجٍ - without any crookedness. The Quran doesn't obscure truth behind layers of complexity. It presents guidance clearly and directly لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ- so that they might become conscious of Allah ﷻ, so that they might protect themselves.

The message is accessible because it's meant to be lived, not just intellectually puzzled over. It's meant to transform hearts and guide actions, and for that purpose, clarity is mercy.

translation: Allah presents an example: a slave owned by quarreling partners and another belonging exclusively to one man - are they equal in comparison? Praise be to Allah! But most of them do not know
commentary:
ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا رَّجُلًا فِيهِ شُرَكَآءُ مُتَشَـٰكِسُونَ وَرَجُلًا سَلَمًا لِّرَجُلٍ هَلْ يَسْتَوِيَانِ مَثَلًا ۚ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ

Allah ﷻ presents a powerful parable: imagine a slave owned by multiple masters who are *mutashakisun* - constantly quarreling and harsh with him. One master tells him to work on one task, another demands something different, and a third contradicts them both. This slave is perpetually in trouble, never knowing if he's doing the right thing because satisfying one master inevitably angers another.

The word *mutashakis* captures the bad manners and constant disagreement among these partners. They're always at odds about how to use this slave, and he's caught in the middle of their disputes. His situation is one of constant anxiety and confusion.

In contrast, there's another man who is *salaman li-rajul* - belonging to just one master, living in peace. He has clarity about his duties and purpose. He knows exactly what's expected of him and isn't torn between competing demands.

Allah ﷻ asks: *hal yastawiyani mathalan* - are these two equal in their situation? Can someone living in constant confusion and distress be compared to someone who has peace and clarity? The answer is obvious.

This parable directly addresses the concept of shirk (associating partners with Allah ﷻ). Just as the slave with multiple masters lives in perpetual confusion, those who worship multiple gods face an impossible situation. In Greek mythology, for example, pleasing one god might anger another. The polytheists are unknowingly describing their own religious confusion when they worship multiple deities.

The ayah concludes with **الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ** - all praise belongs to Allah ﷻ, the One Master. Having one God simplifies life rather than complicating it. However, **بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ** - most of them don't even realize that this monotheism is actually making life easier for them, not harder.

translation: Indeed, you are to die, and indeed, they are to die
commentary:
إِنَّكَ مَيِّتٌ وَإِنَّهُم مَّيِّتُونَ

This short but powerful ayah addresses the Prophet ﷺ directly: **إِنَّكَ مَيِّتٌ** - you are going to die. There's no way around it. Even the best of creation will experience death.

**وَإِنَّهُم مَّيِّتُونَ** - and they are absolutely going to die too. This statement levels the playing field. Death is the one certainty that unites the believer and the disbeliever, the prophet and his opponents. No one escapes this reality.

translation: Then indeed you, on the Day of Resurrection, before your Lord, will dispute
commentary:
ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ تَخْتَصِمُونَ

After death comes resurrection: **ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ**- then, on the Day of Resurrection, **عِند رَبِّكُمْ تَخْتَصِمُونَ**  - before your Master, all of you will be rehashing this debate.

The disputes that took place in this world - about the truth of the message, about monotheism versus polytheism, about the Prophet's ﷺ claim - will all be brought up again. But this time, the setting will be before Allah ﷻ Himself, where the truth will be undeniable and the consequences final.

translation: So who is more unjust than one who lies about Allah and denies the truth when it has come to him? Is there not in Hell a residence for the disbelievers
commentary:
۞ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن كَذَبَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ وَكَذَّبَ بِٱلصِّدْقِ إِذْ جَآءَهُۥٓ ۚ أَلَيْسَ فِى جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ

The ayah poses a rhetorical question: **فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ** - who can be a worse wrongdoer? The answer: someone who does two things simultaneously.

First, **مِمَّن كَذَبَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ** - they fabricate lies about Allah ﷻ. They make up false claims about what He said or wants.

Second, **وَكَذَّبَ بِٱلصِّدْقِ إِذْ جَآءَهُۥٓ** - they call the truth a lie when it comes to them. Despite recognizing the truth, they deliberately reject it.

This is the ultimate form of wrong: inventing falsehood and rejecting truth at the same time.

The ayah then asks: **أَلَيْسَ فِى جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ** - isn't in Hellfire enough of a place to stay for the disbelievers?

The word **مَثْوًى** carries a powerful meaning. It doesn't just mean a place to stay temporarily; it implies a dwelling where generation after generation resides. In the context of Hellfire, it means not only staying for multiple generations but staying alongside multiple generations - the accumulated disbelievers throughout history, all gathered together in their punishment.

translation: And the one who has brought the truth and [they who] believed in it - those are the righteous
commentary:
وَٱلَّذِى جَآءَ بِٱلصِّدْقِ وَصَدَّقَ بِهِۦٓ ۙ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ

This ayah describes two dimensions of faith embodied in the Prophet **وَٱلَّذِى جَآءَ بِٱلصِّدْقِ**  - the one who brought the truth,**وَصَدَّقَ بِهِۦٓ** - and the one who confirmed it himself.

The Prophet ﷺ wasn't merely a messenger delivering words he didn't believe. He had to take his own leap of faith. When he first experienced the revelation through Angel Jibreel (AS), he was in shock. He had to process whether he was losing his mind or whether this was truly divine guidance. He himself had to believe and confirm the truth of what was being revealed to him.

**أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ** - those who bring truth and confirm it, these are the real people of taqwa. This ayah completes the connection back to Ayah 28, where the purpose of the Quran's clarity was - so that they may develop taqwa.

translation: They will have whatever they desire with their Lord. That is the reward of the doers of good
commentary:
لَهُم مَّا يَشَآءُونَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ

For these people of taqwa: **لَهُم مَّا يَشَآءُونَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ** - they will have whatever they want with their Master.

This isn't just about material rewards. It's about the fulfillment of every desire in the presence of Allah .**ذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ** - that is the compensation for those who excel, those who strive for excellence in their faith and actions.

translation: That Allah may remove from them the worst of what they did and reward them their due for the best of what they used to do
commentary:
لِيُكَفِّرَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ أَسْوَأَ ٱلَّذِى عَمِلُوا۟ وَيَجْزِيَهُمْ أَجْرَهُم بِأَحْسَنِ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ

This ayah reveals Allah's ﷻ extraordinary mercy: **لِيُكَفِّرَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ أَسْوَأَ ٱلَّذِى عَمِلُوا۟** - so that Allah ﷻ can bury away from them the worst of the things they did.

Being a person of taqwa doesn't mean you're an angel who never made mistakes. Even the righteous have done some bad things. Allah ﷻ promises to take the worst of those deeds and bury them completely, so they can't even be found.

But there's more: **وَيَجْزِيَهُمْ أَجْرَهُم بِأَحْسَنِ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ**- and He will pay them their reward in full based on the very best of what they used to do.

Imagine you performed a million good deeds, but the quality varied. Most were mediocre - maybe 30% effort. But one deed was performed with 99% excellence. Allah ﷻ takes that one outstanding deed and raises the bar, elevating all your other deeds to that level. He pays you in full for all of them based on the best you ever achieved. This is divine generosity beyond human comprehension.

translation: Is not Allah sufficient for His Servant [Prophet Muhammad]? And [yet], they threaten you with those [they worship] other than Him. And whoever Allah leaves astray - for him there is no guide
commentary:
أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِكَافٍ عَبْدَهُۥ ۖ وَيُخَوِّفُونَكَ بِٱلَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِۦ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍ

After describing all these blessings, Allah ﷻ asks: **أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِكَافٍ عَبْدَهُۥ** - isn't Allah ﷻ enough for His slave?

Why would anyone seek other gods when Allah ﷻ is taking care of them now, will reward them without restriction in the Hereafter, and will bury their sins? What more could anyone possibly need?

Yet **وَيُخَوِّفُونَكَ بِٱلَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِۦ** - they try to scare you by means of those that are other than Him. The polytheists would threaten people: "If you abandon your gods, the curse of this idol will come upon you. The spirit of that one will haunt you. Your children will be deformed. Terrible things will happen."

They used superstition and fear-mongering to keep people trapped in false worship.

But Allah ﷻ concludes with a sobering reality: **وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍ** - whoever Allah ﷻ allows to be misguided, there will never be anyone capable of guiding him.

This is a profound statement. Someone could live in Makkah in the company of the Prophet ﷺ - the most incredible teacher ever sent to humanity. They could hear the Quran - the best possible message ever given. They could speak Arabic better than any scholar who ever lived, understanding every nuance of the language. Yet if they shut their minds to truth and let superstition rule their hearts, even all these advantages won't help them find guidance.

This reminds us that guidance isn't merely about access to information or eloquence in language. Even Abu Jahl, who knew Arabic at a level no contemporary scholar could match, remained lost. Guidance requires an open heart willing to accept truth when it arrives, no matter how clear the message or how perfect the teacher.

translation: And whoever Allah guides - for him there is no misleader. Is not Allah Exalted in Might and Owner of Retribution
commentary:
وَمَن يَهْدِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِن مُّضِلٍّ ۗ أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِعَزِيزٍ ذِى ٱنتِقَامٍ

The parallel structure continues: whoever Allah ﷻ commits to guidance, no one can ever misguide them. This establishes the absolute sovereignty of Allah ﷻ over hearts and souls. No matter how many false gods the mushrikeen invoke, no matter how many threats they issue, they cannot change what Allah ﷻ has decreed.

أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِعَزِيزٍ ذِى ٱنتِقَامٍ - Isn't Allah ﷻ the Ultimate Authority, possessor of the power to take vengeance? The phrase ذِى ٱنتِقَامٍ  is particularly meaningful. Allah ﷻ doesn't describe Himself as المنتقم , "the Avenger" - the one who is known for taking revenge. Rather, He uses ذِى ٱنتِقَامٍ - the one who possesses the power and ability to take revenge.

This subtle difference carries an element of mercy. When someone is known for revenge, you expect it from them constantly. But when someone merely has the capability, you fear their power while also recognizing they may choose mercy. This description instills both awe and hope.

translation: And if you asked them, "Who created the heavens and the earth?" they would surely say, "Allah." Say, "Then have you considered what you invoke besides Allah? If Allah intended me harm, are they removers of His harm; or if He intended me mercy, are they withholders of His mercy?" Say, "Sufficient for me is Allah; upon Him [alone] rely the [wise] reliers
commentary:
وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ ٱللَّهُ ۚ قُلْ أَفَرَءَيْتُم مَّا تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِنْ أَرَادَنِىَ ٱللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ هَلْ هُنَّ كَـٰشِفَـٰتُ ضُرِّهِۦٓ أَوْ أَرَادَنِى بِرَحْمَةٍ هَلْ هُنَّ مُمْسِكَـٰتُ رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۚ قُلْ حَسْبِىَ ٱللَّهُ ۖ عَلَيْهِ يَتَوَكَّلُ ٱلْمُتَوَكَّلُونَ

If you were to ask them who created the skies and the Earth, they would absolutely respond: Allah. Even the mushrikeen, despite calling upon multiple gods, acknowledged that the ultimate Creator is Allah ﷻ alone. This admission exposes the contradiction at the heart of their shirk.

The Prophet ﷺ is commanded to make himself the case study, following the methodology of Ibrahim (AS) who would point to himself in debates: قُلْ أَفَرَءَيْتُم - Say: Have you really thought about what you're calling upon besides Allah? If Allah ﷻ intends harm for me, can your gods remove that harm? If He intends mercy for me, can they withhold His mercy?

These rhetorical questions dismantle the entire logic of intermediary worship. If these deities cannot override Allah's ﷻ will in the smallest matter, what purpose do they serve? They offer no protection from harm, no guarantee of blessing. They are utterly powerless before the One they themselves admit is the Creator.

قُلْ حَسْبِىَ ٱللَّهُ - Say: Allah is sufficient for me. This echoes the opening of ayah 36: أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِكَافٍ عَبْدَهُۥ. The servant declares what Allah ﷻ asked rhetorically. It's a beautiful circle - Allah ﷻ affirms His sufficiency, and His slave confirms it: حَسْبِىَ ٱللَّهُ. The word حَسْبِ means "absolutely enough" - I need nothing beyond this.

عَلَيْهِ يَتَوَكَّلُ ٱلْمُتَوَكَّلُونَ - Upon Him, those who truly place trust can only really place their trust. Everyone who places trust elsewhere will eventually be disappointed. Only He never fails those who rely on Him.

translation: Say, "O my people, work according to your position, [for] indeed, I am working; and you are going to know
commentary:
قُلْ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنِّى عَـٰمِلٌ ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ

Tell them: O my people, do whatever you can from your position. This is the same warning Prophet Nuh (AS) issued to his nation. The phrase عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ refers to their position of authority, their control over the people, their ability to bend rules and manipulate circumstances against the believers. Exercise all the power you have. Use your political influence, your social capital, your wealth and connections.

إِنِّى عَـٰمِلٌ - I too am putting myself to work. I'm doing my part as well. There's a calm confidence in this statement. The Prophet ﷺ isn't intimidated by their threats or their power plays. He's simply going to continue his mission.

فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ - Soon you're going to find out. This is a threat wrapped in certainty. You'll discover who was truly on the right path.

translation: To whom will come a torment disgracing him and on whom will descend an enduring punishment
commentary:
مَن يَأْتِيهِ عَذَابٌ يُخْزِيهِ وَيَحِلُّ عَلَيْهِ عَذَابٌ مُّقِيمٌ

You're going to find out who will receive punishment that humiliates him, and upon whom permanent punishment will be unleashed. The word يَحِلُّ means to be opened, unraveled, made to descend upon someone. This punishment doesn't just touch them briefly - it settles in and stays.

مُّقِيمٌ - Permanent, established, unending. The word implies something that doesn't go away, that remains constant. This stands in stark contrast to the temporary authority and power the disbelievers currently wield.

translation: Indeed, We sent down to you the Book for the people in truth. So whoever is guided - it is for [the benefit of] his soul; and whoever goes astray only goes astray to its detriment. And you are not a manager over them
commentary:
إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ لِلنَّاسِ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ فَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ فَلِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن ضَلَّ فَإِنَّمَا يَضِلُّ عَلَيْهَا ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِوَكِيلٍ

We have sent down to you the Book for the people, with purpose, with truth. This addresses the Prophet ﷺ directly, reminding him that he has been given everything necessary. The revelation is complete, purposeful, and truthful. There's no deficiency on his end.

فَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ فَلِنَفْسِهِۦ - Whoever commits himself to guidance does so for his own benefit. The phrase فَلِنَفْسِهِۦ emphasizes that guidance benefits the guided person themselves. They aren't doing Allah ﷻ any favors by accepting truth.

وَمَن ضَلَّ فَإِنَّمَا يَضِلُّ عَلَيْهَا - And whoever chooses to go astray does so against his own self. The misguidance harms only the misguided. Their rejection doesn't diminish the truth or harm the message.

وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِوَكِيلٍ - You are not in charge of managing their affairs. You cannot control people when they choose the wrong path. This is a powerful reminder to the Prophet ﷺ himself. As much as he loves his uncle Abu Talib, as wise and intelligent as that man is, the Prophet ﷺ cannot force him to accept Islam. It's frustrating, but Allah ﷻ tells His Messenger: you're not responsible for their choices. You have the Book, which was sent with clear purpose. You don't need more proof. But if people are determined to reject it, you can only do your part and remind them. That's it.

translation: Allah takes the souls at the time of their death, and those that do not die [He takes] during their sleep. Then He keeps those for which He has decreed death and releases the others for a specified term. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought
commentary:
ٱللَّهُ يَتَوَفَّى ٱلْأَنفُسَ حِينَ مَوْتِهَا وَٱلَّتِى لَمْ تَمُتْ فِى مَنَامِهَا ۖ فَيُمْسِكُ ٱلَّتِى قَضَىٰ عَلَيْهَا ٱلْمَوْتَ وَيُرْسِلُ ٱلْأُخْرَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ

Allah ﷻ takes the anfus - the souls, the lives, the breath of people - at the time of their death. The word الْأَنفُسَ is significant. نَفْس means to breathe, and it refers to both physical and spiritual breath. There are two forms of life: the physical body breathes, and the unseen soul has its own form of breath. This is why the Quran uses nafs here.

Notice the eloquence: if Allah ﷻ had said "Allah takes souls at the time of their death," the statement would be redundant. But by using the general term أَلْأَنفُسَ, the ayah can then specify: حِينَ مَوْتِهَا - at the time of their death, وَٱلَّتِى لَمْ تَمُتْ فِى مَنَامِهَا - and those who have not died, He takes them during their sleep.

This is remarkable. The Quran is explicitly distinguishing between موت (death) and توفى (taking away). When you fall asleep and your eyes slowly close, that's an angel putting you into a spiritual coma. Your soul leaves your body and is taken to the realm of the unseen. This is why sometimes you experience true dreams - because your soul has been transported to a place where communication from Allah ﷻ can reach you.

فَيُمْسِكُ ٱلَّتِى قَضَىٰ عَلَيْهَا ٱلْمَوْتَ - Now that the soul has left the body, Allah ﷻ decides: this one, I'm not sending back. His time has come. He's going to die in his sleep. The angels are essentially asking every single night: Should we let this person return? And for the one whose decree of death has come, Allah ﷻ says: Hold this one back.

وَيُرْسِلُ ٱلْأُخْرَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى - He sends the others back until their appointed time. Every night, there's a decision made about whether you wake up. This back-and-forth of your soul leaving and returning happens again and again until the given time arrives, and then it's final.

This is why when we wake up, we say: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ - All praise belongs to Allah who gave us life after He caused us to die, and to Him is the resurrection. We're acknowledging that we died temporarily and were brought back. That "pulling the plug" decision we think only happens in hospitals when someone is in a coma - should we resuscitate or let them go - that conversation is happening every single night with every human being.

إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ - In this are miraculous signs for people who engage in deep thought. Someone who truly reflects on this reality cannot go to sleep the same way ever again. You'd think: What did I do with my life today? Is this the day Allah ﷻ will tell the angels to keep me asleep? The next time I wake up, will it be on the Day of Judgment?

translation: Or have they taken other than Allah as intercessors? Say, "Even though they do not possess [power over] anything, nor do they reason
commentary:
أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ شُفَعَآءَ ۚ قُلْ أَوَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَعْقِلُونَ

Have they taken intercessors besides Allah? Shafa'a - intercession, protection, someone to make their case before the Judge. They imagine these intermediaries as connections, like having a powerful family or insider access.

قُلْ أَوَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَعْقِلُونَ - Say: Even though they don't control anything and don't understand anything? This rhetorical question exposes the absurdity. These so-called intercessors - whether idols, false deities, or created beings elevated beyond their station - have no authority and no comprehension. Yet people place their hope in them.

This includes those who hold onto their religious symbols superstitiously, staring at pictures before sleep expecting protection, performing rituals around objects hoping for intercession. But these things understand nothing and control nothing.

translation: Say, "To Allah belongs [the right to allow] intercession entirely. To Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. Then to Him you will be returned
commentary:
قُل لِّلَّهِ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةُ جَمِيعًا ۖ لَّهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ

Tell them: The power to grant intercession belongs to Allah ﷻ entirely. All of it, completely, without exception. No one intercedes except by His permission, according to His will, within His framework.

لَّهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - To Him alone belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the Earth. He is the ultimate authority, the final decision-maker.

ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ - Then to Him alone you will be returned. The word ثُمَّ is beautiful here because people imagine they'll go to some sub-department first, some intermediate station. Like a lawyer who says, "You don't even have to come to court, I'll handle the paperwork for you, just stay home and relax." But Allah ﷻ makes clear: No, you're going straight to Him. There's no preliminary hearing, no intermediate processing. You will stand directly before the Judge.

translation: And when Allah is mentioned alone, the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter shrink with aversion, but when those [worshipped] other than Him are mentioned, immediately they rejoice
commentary:
وَإِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ ٱشْمَأَزَّتْ قُلُوبُ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَإِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ

When Allah ﷻ alone is mentioned - when only Allah ﷻ is being discussed, with no other angel, no story attached, just Allah ﷻ - ٱشْمَأَزَّتْ قُلُوبُ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ - the hearts of those who don't truly believe in the Hereafter become disturbed, unsettled. They experience a kind of unrest, their hearts skip a beat. They think: Why is this conversation so boring? Why isn't anyone talking about something else?

You'll witness this dynamic in gatherings. When the discussion turns to Allah ﷻ alone, some people become uncomfortable. They think the conversation is dry, lacking flavor. They'll even interrupt: "Why don't we talk about something else? Can we take it easy with this topic?"

وَإِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ - But when others besides Him are mentioned, suddenly they become happy and engaged. The conversation comes alive for them. Talk about sports, politics, entertainment, celebrities - anyone and anything except Allah ﷻ - and they're animated, interested, contributing.

This is about attitude. A person of true tawheed isn't just someone who intellectually believes in One God. Their entire disposition is different. Their conversations reflect their priorities. They don't find discussions about Allah ﷻ tedious or uncomfortable. This ayah reveals that monotheism has practical, observable implications in daily life.

translation: Say, "O Allah, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, You will judge between your servants concerning that over which they used to differ
commentary:
قُلِ ٱللَّهُمَّ فَاطِرَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ عَـٰلِمَ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ أَنتَ تَحْكُمُ بَيْنَ عِبَادِكَ فِى مَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ

Say: O Allah, Fatir of the heavens and the Earth. فَاطِرَ means the One who initiates creation, the original Designer and Molder. This isn't just about creating - it's about originating something that never existed before, designing its very nature and function.

عَـٰلِمَ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ - Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the hidden and the manifest. You know what we cannot see and what we can see, what is concealed and what is apparent.

أَنتَ تَحْكُمُ بَيْنَ عِبَادِكَ فِى مَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ - You are the One who will judge between Your servants in all that they used to disagree about. This is both a prayer and a declaration of trust. The Prophet ﷺ is essentially saying: These disputes, these conflicts, these fundamental disagreements about truth - You will settle them all. I'm leaving the judgment to You.

translation: And if those who did wrong had all that is in the earth entirely and the like of it with it, they would [attempt to] ransom themselves thereby from the worst of the punishment on the Day of Resurrection. And there will appear to them from Allah that which they had not taken into account
commentary:
وَلَوْ أَنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا وَمِثْلَهُۥ مَعَهُۥ لَٱفْتَدَوْا۟ بِهِۦ مِن سُوٓءِ ٱلْعَذَابِ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ وَبَدَا لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يَكُونُوا۟ يَحْتَسِبُونَ

If those who committed wrong owned everything on Earth entirely, and then another Earth's worth of treasures on top of it - if they possessed the value of two planets - لَٱفْتَدَوْا۟ بِهِۦ مِن سُوٓءِ ٱلْعَذَابِ they would give it all away as ransom to escape the punishment.

This reveals a powerful psychological truth: when someone is about to be tortured, what are they willing to pay to avoid it? Everything. Take my house, take my savings, take it all - just don't hurt me. They won't negotiate or bargain. If they owned everything on the planet, they wouldn't say "Is ten thousand enough? Is my house enough?" They'd offer every last thing without hesitation.

وَبَدَا لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يَكُونُوا۟ يَحْتَسِبُونَ - And then something will appear before them from Allah ﷻ that they could never have imagined. No horror movie producer has ever created anything that captures what these people will witness. No description of Hell, no artistic rendering or animation, has done justice to these horrors. It hasn't even entered human imagination, the reality of what they're about to experience.

translation: And there will appear to them the evils they had earned, and they will be enveloped by what they used to ridicule
commentary:
وَبَدَا لَهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا كَسَبُوا۟ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ

The evils of what they earned will appear before them. This teaches us something profound: evil deeds take physical form. Your actions transform into an ugly, tangible thing that you have to face. You see your deeds materialized - "I am your lie. I am your theft. I am your betrayal." The sin becomes a creature, a reality you must confront face to face. This is what I did. This is what I made for myself.

وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ - And the very thing they used to mock and ridicule surrounds them, takes hold of them. All those warnings they laughed at, all the consequences they dismissed with jokes and sarcasm - now those realities encompass them completely. There's no escape, no distance. What they mocked has become their inescapable prison.

translation: And when adversity touches man, he calls upon Us; then when We bestow on him a favor from Us, he says, "I have only been given it because of [my] knowledge." Rather, it is a trial, but most of them do not know
commentary:
فَإِذَا مَسَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ ضُرٌّ دَعَانَا ثُمَّ إِذَا خَوَّلْنَـٰهُ نِعْمَةً مِّنَّا قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أُوتِيتُهُۥ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍۭ ۚ بَلْ هِىَ فِتْنَةٌ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ

When harm touches the human being, he calls on Us. This parallels the Day of Judgment, doesn't it? When trouble comes, what's the natural human response? To call on Allah ﷻ. In that moment of desperation, all false gods are forgotten. There's an instinctive recognition of who truly has power.

ثُمَّ إِذَا خَوَّلْنَـٰهُ نِعْمَةً مِّنَّا - Then when We wrap him in blessing, when We fold him up and layer him with favor after favor, luxury after luxury, comfort upon comfort - قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أُوتِيتُهُۥ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍۭ - he says: I acquired this through knowledge. Through some expertise, some insider information, some smart move I made.

"I invested in the right stock because I had good information. I chose the right major because my guidance counselor gave me solid advice. I made strategic decisions that paid off. I networked effectively. I worked hard and smart. That's why I'm successful - because of what I knew and what I did."

This person is already writing the book: "How to Become Successful: Insider Secrets." They attribute everything to their own intelligence, their own capability, their own knowledge.

بَلْ هِىَ فِتْنَةٌ - But no, it is a trial, a test. That blessing, that success, that wealth - it's not a validation of your superiority or proof of your wisdom. It's a fitna, an examination. Will you remain grateful? Will you acknowledge the source? Or will you become deluded by your own perceived competence?

وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ - But most of them don't know. They don't recognize that their blessings are a test. They mistake divine provision for personal achievement, confuse Allah's ﷻ favor with their own merit. And in that ignorance, they fail the very test they don't realize they're taking.

translation: Those before them had already said it, but they were not availed by what they used to earn
commentary:
**قَدْ قَالَهَا ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَمَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ**

The attitude described in the previous ayah is nothing new. People throughout history have made this same claim, attributing their success to their own abilities. Allah ﷻ reminds us that previous nations said the exact same thing.

And what happened to them? **فَمَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ** - "None of what they had earned came to aid them at all." All their accumulated wealth, knowledge, and accomplishments proved worthless when Allah's ﷻ decree came. Their earnings made them neither independent nor safe from the consequences of their arrogance.

translation: And the evil consequences of what they earned struck them. And those who have wronged of these [people] will be afflicted by the evil consequences of what they earned; and they will not cause failure
commentary:
**فَأَصَابَهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا كَسَبُوا۟ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ سَيُصِيبُهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا كَسَبُوا۟ وَمَا هُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ**

The evil consequences of their actions struck them directly. The imagery here is powerful: **أَصَابَهُمْ**  comes from the root meaning to hit a target. Their sins came back to them like arrows or missiles finding their mark.

This connects to a crucial reality about wealth and power. When someone accumulates significant resources, they believe they can defend against any problem. They think they can throw money at difficulties, dispatch employees to handle crises, or rely on layers of security to keep trouble at bay. Their defenses seem impenetrable - layer after layer of protection.

But Allah ﷻ says the evil consequences will target them precisely. **سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا كَسَبُوا** will penetrate all those luxuries and defenses and strike them exactly where they are. The word **مُعْجِزِينَ**  comes from عجز, meaning to overpower, cancel out, or nullify. Just as anti-aircraft fire attempts to neutralize incoming missiles, these people think their wealth can neutralize any threat. But they will not be able to overpower or defend against Allah's ﷻ decree.

The ayah then makes this warning contemporary: **وَٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ** - "And those who do wrong among these people" - meaning this same fate awaits the wrongdoers in every generation, including the listeners of this Quran. History repeats itself when people fail to learn its lessons.

translation: Do they not know that Allah extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]? Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe
commentary:
**أَوَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ**

After describing those surrounded by luxury who forget Allah ﷻ, this ayah poses a rhetorical question: Haven't they realized already? Don't they know that it is Allah ﷻ who expands provisions - **يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ** - opening doors for the second promotion, the successful business venture, all the opportunities that came their way?

And it is He ﷻ who constricts those opportunities - **وَيَقْدِرُ** - closing doors, restricting possibilities. Both expansion and constriction come from Him ﷻ alone.

**إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ** - "Indeed in that are signs for people who want to believe." The patterns of provision throughout human life and history serve as clear evidence of Allah's ﷻ control for those who are willing to see and acknowledge it.

translation: Say, "O My servants who have transgressed against themselves [by sinning], do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful
commentary:
**۞ قُلْ يَـٰعِبَادِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا۟ مِن رَّحْمَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَغْفِرُ ٱلذُّنُوبَ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ**

The previous ayahs discussed people experiencing extreme luxury who forget Allah ﷻ. Now the focus shifts to those who have lost that comfort. Perhaps life was going well, but then circumstances deteriorated. The marriage began falling apart, children stopped listening, the bank denied the loan, customers left, the best employee quit, the business struggled. Everything started unraveling.

What happens when things fall apart? The person turns to Allah ﷻ and makes dua - this was described earlier in the surah. But when someone makes dua, they want immediate results. The store owner in Pakistan or Egypt prays desperately during the busy season, hoping sales will turn around. Someone applies to their dream college or company, making constant dua. They visit for an interview and fall in love with everything - the building, the lobby, the parking lot. They drive their friends past it: "That's where I work!" 

Then three weeks later, they're laid off. The newly hired are the first to go. Or the college rejection comes. Or months pass with no job offers despite applying everywhere, talking to every contact, submitting applications even to places they're overqualified for. Nothing comes through.

It's in this state of prolonged difficulty that Allah ﷻ addresses humanity with one of the Quran's most merciful statements: **قُلْ يَـٰعِبَادِىَ** - "Say: O My slaves." This address encompasses all human beings, not just believers. **ٱلَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ** - "Those who have transgressed against themselves" - those who have gone overboard in sin, who have made serious mistakes, who have done things they shouldn't have done.

**لَا تَقْنَطُوا۟ مِن رَّحْمَةِ ٱللَّهِ** - "Do not despair of Allah's mercy." The word قَنَطَ  means to lose all hope, to become completely pessimistic, to see only the negative in everything, to view the glass as perpetually half empty. It's that crushing feeling where you think, "I'm applying for jobs, but I don't think I'll get one. I know it won't work out."

This applies to both worldly and spiritual matters. Someone may think: "I've sinned too much. I've done terrible things. How could Allah ﷻ show me mercy after what I've done? It wasn't even Shaytan's fault - I can't blame anyone but myself." When these thoughts consume a person, they feel beyond redemption.

Allah ﷻ demolishes this despair with an emphatic declaration: Don't you dare lose hope in Allah's mercy. Elsewhere in the Quran, only the wrongdoers and disbelievers lose hope in Allah's ﷻ mercy. Don't think you're too far gone. Don't weigh the volume of your sins without weighing the vastness of Allah's ﷻ mercy.
