As one scholar beautifully explained: A Muslim must weigh two things. On one hand, how bad are my sins? Give them a conceptual weight - a million pounds, twenty million tons. On the other hand, how vast is my Lord's mercy? Now compare: how can you possibly conclude that your sins outweigh His ﷻ mercy? You cannot lose hope that His ﷻ mercy will overcome your sins.

Many people lose hope not because of their own assessment, but because of how other people treat them. Islamic discourse in much of the Muslim world has become imbalanced. The prophetic message contained both good news  and warning , but modern sermons often emphasize only warnings. People hear constantly: "This is haram, that is haram, you're doing everything wrong, there's so much shamelessness, you're heading to Hellfire."

This relentless negativity leaves people depressed and hopeless. They think, "Everything I hear tells me I'm guaranteed Hell. Where's the mercy? All I hear is how wrong my life is." Over time, this creates a dangerous psychology: people focus only on their sins, and sins lead to Hell, so they conclude Allah ﷻ won't show them mercy. They give up entirely. "I'm burning anyway, so I might as well party on my way down."

Like a soldier who knows he's going to die in battle tomorrow and decides to live it up tonight, the person who loses hope goes off the deep end. Drugs, alcohol, immorality become easy to justify: "What difference does it make? I'm doomed anyway."

This is why Allah ﷻ makes this powerful declaration: **إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَغْفِرُ ٱلذُّنُوبَ جَمِيعًا** - "Indeed, Allah forgives all sins completely." Not some sins. Not small sins. All sins, entirely.

**إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ** - "Indeed He is the Ghafur, the Rahim." This pairing of names is deliberate and profound. Ghafur (exceedingly forgiving) uses the pattern that indicates intensity but not permanence. Rahim (always merciful) indicates permanence without the emphasis on intensity.

In other words: He ﷻ is extremely forgiving, and He ﷻ is always merciful. If Allah ﷻ said He is "always forgiving," people might abuse this by sinning freely, assuming forgiveness is automatic. But He ﷻ is extremely forgiving - indicating that forgiveness comes when proper conditions are met - and always merciful, meaning the door to forgiveness remains perpetually open.

His ﷻ mercy is that He never closes the door to repentance. No matter how many mistakes you've made, His ﷻ love and compassion haven't disappeared. That door remains open. It's like a mother whose son has disappointed her deeply, hurt her feelings, and left. Even if he returns after ten, twenty, or thirty years, when he comes back crying and says, "Mom, I'm sorry," she still embraces him. That bond - the womb's connection (رحم), from which we get Rahim - never breaks. That's one creation of Allah ﷻ. How much more so with Allah ﷻ Himself, from whom all mercy originates?

translation: And return [in repentance] to your Lord and submit to Him before the punishment comes upon you; then you will not be helped
commentary:
**وَأَنِيبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ وَأَسْلِمُوا۟ لَهُۥ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ثُمَّ لَا تُنصَرُونَ**

Having heard that you shouldn't lose hope, how should you proceed? **أَنِيبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ** - "Turn back to your Master." Spiritually return to Him ﷻ, come back to Him ﷻ. The word إنابة implies you've walked away. You stopped talking to Him ﷻ, stopped thinking about Him ﷻ, didn't consider His ﷻ presence even though He ﷻ was right there with you.

We will encounter this concept again in this passage - the one sitting right next to you . Some people spend hours on a train ignoring the person beside them. Similarly, we go through entire days and nights, at home and at work, without acknowledging Allah's ﷻ presence.

Imagine sitting next to someone you know well - your mother, your spouse - and deliberately ignoring them for hours. If you ignore someone in a gathering while talking to others, it's deeply offensive. When three people sit together and two talk while completely ignoring the third, that's hurtful. Now consider: whenever we sit in any gathering, who is the Third? The Prophet ﷺ taught us: **هُوَ الثَّالِثُ** - "He is the Third." How often do we ignore Him ﷻ?

If you ignore anyone else for a prolonged period, they eventually leave, saying, "These people don't include me." It's like playing basketball for hours without ever getting passed the ball - eventually you quit because you're not being engaged. But Allah ﷻ is different. Despite being ignored, He ﷻ says: Turn back to Me now. Just turn back.

**وَأَسْلِمُوا۟ لَهُۥ** - "And submit to Him." This time, show proper respect. Give yourself up before Him ﷻ. Don't bring your pride, your ego, your assumptions about yourself. Just completely surrender.

**مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ثُمَّ لَا تُنصَرُونَ** - "Before punishment comes your way, then you will not be helped." Allah's ﷻ forgiveness is vast and extreme up until a point. Right now, the doors are open. Come begging for forgiveness and you'll find those doors wide open. But a time will come when you'll come begging and find those doors closed. Act before that time arrives.

translation: And follow the best of what was revealed to you from your Lord before the punishment comes upon you suddenly while you do not perceive
commentary:
**وَٱتَّبِعُوٓا۟ أَحْسَنَ مَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ بَغْتَةً وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَشْعُرُونَ**

What does it mean practically to turn back to Allah ﷻ and submit? This ayah provides the answer: **ٱتَّبِعُوٓا۟ أَحْسَنَ مَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُم** - "Follow the best of what has been revealed to you from your Master."

This phrase carries multiple meanings. First, of all revelations that have come to humanity, follow the best - follow Islam, follow the final Messenger ﷺ, follow the Quran. Second, take from the revelation that which calls you to إحسان  - excellence, beauty, spiritual excellence. When you learn something from the Quran, implement it in your life. When you hear something in the khutbah that resonates, put it into practice. When you make dua, do so with excellence. Extract the beautiful teachings from revelation and actively incorporate them into your daily existence.

**مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ بَغْتَةً وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَشْعُرُونَ** - "Before the punishment comes to you suddenly while you perceive not." The word بَغْتَةً emphasizes unexpectedness - out of nowhere, without warning. You won't even know what hit you, you won't have any idea what's happening.

translation: Lest a soul should say, "Oh [how great is] my regret over what I neglected in regard to Allah and that I was among the mockers
commentary:
**أَن تَقُولَ نَفْسٌ يَـٰحَسْرَتَىٰ عَلَىٰ مَا فَرَّطتُ فِى جَنۢبِ ٱللَّهِ وَإِن كُنتُ لَمِنَ ٱلسَّـٰخِرِينَ**

This ayah describes the state of a soul on the Day of Judgment, filled with overwhelming regret. The expression **يَـٰحَسْرَتَىٰ** is grammatically remarkable. The original form would be يَا حَسْرَتِي - "O my regret!" But the person cannot even complete the word properly. 

The letter ي requires you to stretch your mouth in something resembling a smile, while ت does not. In this state of anguish, the person cannot even form the sounds correctly - the grief is so consuming that proper speech becomes impossible. They can only cry out: **يَـٰحَسْرَتَىٰ** - an incomplete, broken expression of devastation.

حَسْرَة specifically means regret over something that cannot be undone - crying over spilled milk, anguish over lost opportunities that will never return. The person wails: "What have I done? What did I do?"

**عَلَىٰ مَا فَرَّطتُ فِى جَنۢبِ ٱللَّهِ** - "Over what I neglected regarding Allah" or more literally, "in the company of Allah." The word فَرَّطَ means to fall short, to fail to meet expectations, to neglect what should have been done. 

The phrase **فِى جَنۢبِ ٱللَّهِ** is profound. جَنۢب means "side" or "company" - like someone sitting right next to you. Allah ﷻ was always right there, always in my company, always present. And still, I kept falling short, again and again and again. All I had to do was turn back to Him ﷻ, acknowledge His ﷻ presence, recognize He ﷻ was right there with me all along.

But I didn't. And now it's too late.

translation: Or [lest] it say, "If only Allah had guided me, I would have been among the righteous
commentary:
**أَوْ تَقُولَ لَوْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هَدَىٰنِى لَكُنتُ مِنَ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ**

The ayah presents a second type of person, one who refuses to take responsibility even on Judgment Day. Instead of admitting fault, this person says, "If only Allah ﷻ had guided me, I would have been among the righteous."

This is the excuse of predestination twisted into blame-shifting. "It's not my fault - Allah ﷻ didn't guide me. I'm not in the driver's seat. Allah ﷻ guides whoever He wants, and apparently that wasn't me."

Sound familiar? People make this excuse today. They invoke divine decree to justify their choices: "Allah ﷻ already knows what's going to happen. He decreed everything. If I'm doing haram, it's because it was predestined." They abuse the concept of qadr to evade responsibility for their actions.

The ayah warns us that those who use this excuse now will use the exact same reasoning on the Day of Judgment. But there, it won't hold up any better than it does here. The person claims لَوْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هَدَىٰنِى - "if Allah had guided me" - as though guidance was withheld from them. But guidance was offered. The Quran came. Messengers came. The message was clear. They chose to reject it.

translation: Or [lest] it say when it sees the punishment, "If only I had another turn so I could be among the doers of good
commentary:
**أَوْ تَقُولَ حِينَ تَرَى ٱلْعَذَابَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِى كَرَّةً فَأَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ**

Now we encounter a third type of person - one who changes their story when they actually see the punishment. This person was just blaming Allah ﷻ in the previous ayah, claiming it was all predestination. But when the punishment becomes visible, when it's no longer theoretical, the tune changes completely.

Suddenly this person says, "Wait, let me take that back. It wasn't His fault. It was my fault." And then comes the desperate plea: لَوْ أَنَّ لِى كَرَّةً - "If only I had another chance to go back."

Notice the progression. First there was regret but no responsibility. Then there was blame-shifting to Allah ﷻ. Now there's panic and a desperate wish for a second chance. And not just to be Muslim, not just to be a believer, but to be among المُحْسِنِينَ - those who excel in faith and action, those who practice ihsan.

What was Allah's ﷻ call in this entire passage of Surah Az-Zumar? To become people of ihsan. And now, faced with eternal consequences, this person is ready to promise excellence. "Just give me another shot. I won't blame anyone else. I'm ready to be from the muhsinin now."

But it's too late. The time for action has passed. The doors of repentance close at death, not on Judgment Day.

translation: But yes, there had come to you My verses, but you denied them and were arrogant, and you were among the disbelievers
commentary:
**بَلَىٰ قَدْ جَآءَتْكَ ءَايَـٰتِى فَكَذَّبْتَ بِهَا وَٱسْتَكْبَرْتَ وَكُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ**

Allah ﷻ responds to all these excuses with devastating clarity: بَلَىٰ  - "No, on the contrary." You don't get to rewrite history now. You don't get to pretend you never had a chance.

قَدْ جَآءَتْكَ ءَايَـٰتِى  - "My signs already came to you." This can be understood in multiple ways. Literally, if you want to take it to mean these very ayahs, this conversation was already presented to you in the Quran. You heard this. You knew this was coming. You were warned that you would have these regrets, that you would make these excuses, and you went ahead with the same attitude anyway.

More broadly, the ayat that came were the signs in creation, the Quran itself, the message of the prophets, the warner who came to your community. Guidance was offered repeatedly, in multiple forms.

And what did you do? فَكَذَّبْتَ بِهَا  - "You denied them. You called them lies." You dismissed the truth when it was presented to you.

وَٱسْتَكْبَرْتَ  - "And you were arrogant." You were too proud to submit. Too proud to accept that there was an authority above you, a Creator you would answer to. This pride prevented you from humbling yourself before the truth.

وَكُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ  - "And you were among those who covered the truth." You knew it was true but buried it under layers of denial, mockery, and pride.

translation: And on the Day of Resurrection you will see those who lied about Allah [with] their faces blackened. Is there not in Hell a residence for the arrogant
commentary:
**وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ تَرَى ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَبُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ وُجُوهُهُم مُّسْوَدَّةٌ ۚ أَلَيْسَ فِى جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى لِّلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ**

On the Day of Resurrection, there will be visible markers of people's inner reality. Those who lied against Allah ﷻ - who invented false doctrines, who attributed to Him what He never said, who misrepresented His religion - will have their faces turned black. وُجُوهُهُم مُّسْوَدَّةٌ  - their faces blackened and darkened, carrying the mark of their falsehood.

Then Allah ﷻ poses a rhetorical question: أَلَيْسَ فِى جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى لِّلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ - "Is there not in Hellfire a dwelling for the arrogant?" The word مَثْوًى implies a place of residence for a long duration, generation after generation. This isn't a temporary stop. This is where the arrogant will remain.

Notice that arrogance (kibr) is highlighted as the fundamental problem. It was mentioned in the previous ayah as the reason for rejection, and now it's mentioned again as the quality that earns Hellfire. Pride is what prevented these people from accepting truth, from bowing before their Creator, from admitting their mistakes. And pride is what ultimately destroys them.

translation: And Allah will save those who feared Him by their attainment; no evil will touch them, nor will they grieve
commentary:
**وَيُنَجِّى ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ بِمَفَازَتِهِمْ لَا يَمَسُّهُمُ ٱلسُّوٓءُ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ**

In contrast to the blackened faces of the arrogant, Allah ﷻ now describes how He will rescue those who had taqwa. The verb يُنَجِّى (yunajji) means to save, to rescue - like pulling someone out of danger or drowning.

But notice the unique construction: بِمَفَازَتِهِمْ  - "with their success" or "by means of their success." When you rescue someone from drowning, you use a life vest or throw them a rope. When you save someone from a storm, you provide shelter or a rescue boat. But Allah ﷻ doesn't just rescue the people of taqwa from punishment - He rescues them by placing them in Paradise.

The word مَفَازَة implies ultimate success and triumph. This isn't merely being saved from harm. This is being elevated to the highest achievement. The rescue itself is Paradise. They're not just surviving - they're thriving in a way beyond imagination.

لَا يَمَسُّهُمُ ٱلسُّوٓءُ  - "No harm will touch them." Not even the smallest difficulty or discomfort. And not just freedom from harm, but freedom from grief: وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ  - "nor will they grieve."

This connects back to earlier passages in the Surah about taqwa and its rewards. Now we see the ultimate manifestation of that protection and success.

translation: Allah is the Creator of all things, and He is, over all things, Disposer of affairs
commentary:
**ٱللَّهُ خَـٰلِقُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَكِيلٌ**

Having described the contrasting destinies of the arrogant and the people of taqwa, Allah ﷻ now establishes His absolute authority: ٱللَّهُ خَـٰلِقُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ - "Allah is the Creator of all things." Everything that exists, everything that will happen on the Day of Judgment, every dimension of reality - all of it originates from Him.

وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَكِيلٌ - "And He is the Disposer of all affairs." The word وَكِيلٌ means the one who manages, who takes care of matters, who has everything under control. When you appoint someone as your wakil, you're entrusting them with authority over your affairs.

Allah ﷻ is the Wakil over all of creation. Nothing happens without His knowledge and permission. Every affair, every matter, every outcome is under His complete control. This ayah demolishes the excuse of those who blamed predestination for their choices. Yes, Allah ﷻ is in control - but He also gave you guidance, sent you messengers, gave you a mind to think and a will to choose. His being Wakil doesn't eliminate your responsibility.

translation: To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth. And they who disbelieve in the verses of Allah - it is those who are the losers
commentary:
**لَّهُۥ مَقَالِيدُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ**

Allah ﷻ continues establishing His sovereignty: لَّهُۥ مَقَالِيدُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - "To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth."

The word مَقَالِيدُ is the plural of مِقْلَد, which means key. But there's something deeper in the Arabic root. The root ق-ل-د relates to gathering or filling. The word قَلَادَة  is a necklace - something gathered around the neck. When you say املأ الماء , it means "fill the water." The connection is that a key opens access to something gathered or stored - treasures, vaults, resources.

So مَقَالِيدُ doesn't just mean physical keys. It means Allah ﷻ controls access to all the treasures, resources and realities of the heavens and earth. He controls the rain, the provision, the knowledge, the guidance, the mercy, the punishment - everything is stored in His treasuries, and only He holds the keys.

There's a beautiful hadith where someone asked the Prophet ﷺ what مَقَالِيدُ means, and the Prophet ﷺ responded that no one had asked him that question before, then provided a detailed explanation of these keys to the treasures of the heavens and earth.

Then comes the stark conclusion: وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ - "And those who disbelieve in the signs of Allah - those are the ultimate losers."

When you have access to all of this, when the keys to eternal success are being offered to you through Allah's ﷻ ayat, and you reject them - what could be a greater loss than that?

translation: Say, [O Muhammad], "Is it other than Allah that you order me to worship, O ignorant ones
commentary:
**قُلْ أَفَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ تَأْمُرُوٓنِّىٓ أَعْبُدُ أَيُّهَا ٱلْجَـٰهِلُونَ**

The Prophet ﷺ is now commanded to confront those who tried to pressure him away from pure monotheism: قُلْ - "Say."

أَفَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ تَأْمُرُوٓنِّىٓ أَعْبُدُ  - "Are you actually commanding me to worship other than Allah?" First you tried to scare me with the threats of your false gods. Now you're trying to force me, to command me to worship them?

أَيُّهَا ٱلْجَـٰهِلُونَ  - "O you ignorant ones!" This is a devastating description. After everything that's been explained about Allah's ﷻ sovereignty, His treasures, His control over all affairs, His being the Creator and Disposer of everything - to still suggest worshiping something other than Him is the height of ignorance.

The word جَاهِل doesn't just mean lacking knowledge. It means acting foolishly despite having access to knowledge. It's willful ignorance, the kind that leads to destructive choices.

translation: And it was already revealed to you and to those before you that if you should associate [anything] with Allah, your work would surely become worthless, and you would surely be among the losers
commentary:
**وَلَقَدْ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكَ لَئِنْ أَشْرَكْتَ لَيَحْبَطَنَّ عَمَلُكَ وَلَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ**

Allah ﷻ reminds the Prophet ﷺ and through him, all of us: وَلَقَدْ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكَ - "And it was already revealed to you and to those before you."

This warning isn't new. Every prophet brought the same fundamental message about shirk. Then comes the hypothetical warning: لَئِنْ أَشْرَكْتَ  - "If you were to associate partners with Allah" - and we know this is hypothetical because the Prophet ﷺ would never commit shirk - لَيَحْبَطَنَّ عَمَلُكَ  - "your deeds would be rendered worthless."

The verb حَبِطَ means to be nullified, to become void, to collapse. All the good deeds, all the efforts, all the worship - shirk cancels it all out.

وَلَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ - "And you would surely be among the losers."

The powerful message here is that even a prophet, the best of creation ﷺ, would not be exempt from this principle. This isn't about favoritism or nepotism. The mission is clear: worship Allah ﷻ alone or face the consequences. The Prophet ﷺ was on a mission, accountable before Allah ﷻ like everyone else. He wasn't doing humanity a favor out of benevolence - he was fulfilling his divine mandate, and the threat of shirk applied to him too (hypothetically) to drive home how serious this matter is.

translation: Rather, worship [only] Allah and be among the grateful
commentary:
**بَلِ ٱللَّهَ فَٱعْبُدْ وَكُن مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ**

After the stern warning comes the clear command: بَلِ ٱللَّهَ فَٱعْبُدْ - "Rather, worship Allah alone."

The word بَلِ  indicates a correction or contrast. Instead of all these false alternatives, instead of yielding to pressure from the ignorant, there's only one path: worship Allah ﷻ exclusively.

وَكُن مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ - "And be among the grateful."

This is beautiful. After being commanded to worship Allah ﷻ alone, we're told to be grateful for the opportunity. Be grateful that you get to worship Allah ﷻ. Be grateful that you're among those who have been pulled out of shirk when generations and centuries of people were immersed in it. Be grateful for this clarity, for this guidance, for this pure monotheism.

The imperative is singular (be grateful), but it places you among a group (the grateful ones). You're not alone in this. You're joining a community of people across time who recognized this blessing and responded with gratitude.

translation: They have not appraised Allah with true appraisal, while the earth entirely will be [within] His grip on the Day of Resurrection, and the heavens will be folded in His right hand. Exalted is He and high above what they associate with Him
commentary:
**وَمَا قَدَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِۦ وَٱلْأَرْضُ جَمِيعًا قَبْضَتُهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ مَطْوِيَّـٰتٌۢ بِيَمِينِهِۦ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ**

Now comes one of the most awe-inspiring ayahs in the entire Quran: وَمَا قَدَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِۦ - "They did not appreciate Allah with the appreciation He deserves."

The root ق-د-ر carries meanings of measuring, estimating, appreciating the true value of something. These people who commit shirk, who reject His signs, who act arrogantly - they haven't grasped who Allah ﷻ actually is. They haven't comprehended His true majesty and power.

Then Allah ﷻ gives us a glimpse of His magnificence: وَٱلْأَرْضُ جَمِيعًا قَبْضَتُهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ - "And the entire earth will be in His grip on the Day of Resurrection."

The word قَبْضَة refers to something squeezed or grasped in a fist. Imagine the entire earth - with all its continents, oceans, mountains, everything we consider vast and immense - squeezed in Allah's ﷻ grasp like a small object.

وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ مَطْوِيَّـٰتٌۢ بِيَمِينِهِۦ  - "And the heavens will be folded up in His right hand."

The word مَطْوِيَّـٰتٌ  comes from طَوَى, meaning to fold or roll up - like rolling up a scroll. The heavens, with all their expanse and all the galaxies and cosmic wonders, will be folded up.

The phrase بِيَمِينِهِۦ  - "in His right hand" - is an expression of power and authority. Just as the word يَمِين came earlier in the Surah meaning power and influence ("You used to come to us with your authority"), here it indicates Allah's ﷻ overwhelming power and control.

سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ - "Exalted is He and high above what they associate with Him."

After this mind-bending description of Allah's ﷻ power, we're commanded to declare His perfection and His transcendence above all the false associations people make. How could anyone who truly understands this ayah ever think to put anything alongside Allah ﷻ in worship?

translation: And the Horn will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will fall dead except whom Allah wills. Then it will be blown again, and at once they will be standing, looking on
commentary:
**وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَصَعِقَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۖ ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَىٰ فَإِذَا هُمْ قِيَامٌ يَنظُرُونَ**

Now we're transported to the actual events of the Day of Resurrection: وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ  - "And the trumpet will be blown."

The word صُور (sur) refers to the horn or trumpet that will signal the end of this world and the beginning of the next. This is the first blast.

فَصَعِقَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - "And whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will fall unconscious." The verb صَعِقَ means to fall dead, to collapse, to be struck unconscious.

إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ- "Except whom Allah wills." There will be exceptions - perhaps certain angels, perhaps others. The details are known only to Allah ﷻ.

ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَىٰ - "Then it will be blown again." This is the second blast, the one that brings everyone back to life for judgment.

فَإِذَا هُمْ قِيَامٌ يَنظُرُونَ - "And at once they will be standing, looking around."

The word فَإِذَا  indicates suddenness. One moment there's nothing, then boom - everyone is standing, resurrected, looking around at this new reality. The Day of Judgment has begun.

translation: And the earth will shine with the light of its Lord, and the record [of deeds] will be placed, and the prophets and the witnesses will be brought, and it will be judged between them in truth, and they will not be wronged
commentary:
**وَأَشْرَقَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ بِنُورِ رَبِّهَا وَوُضِعَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ وَجِا۟ىٓءَ بِٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ وَٱلشُّهَدَآءِ وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ**

The scene continues: وَأَشْرَقَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ بِنُورِ رَبِّهَا - "And the earth will shine with the light of its Lord."

This is after the earth has been flattened and transformed. The earth that we know will become something entirely different. On that day, the earth itself will be illuminated by the light of Allah ﷻ. Some scholars explain that on the Day of Judgment, Allah ﷻ will descend and His light will fill the earth. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ will descend with angels in rows upon rows, and the entire creation will be bathed in divine light.

وَوُضِعَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ - "And the record will be placed" or "the book will be laid down." This could refer to the individual records of deeds being distributed, or to the preserved tablet being opened - the complete record of everything that ever happened.

وَجِا۟ىٓءَ بِٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ وَٱلشُّهَدَآءِ - "And the prophets and the witnesses will be brought forth."

The prophets will be there to testify about their nations - whether they conveyed the message, how their people responded. But who are الشُّهَدَآءِ , the witnesses?

These are not just the martyrs (though that's one meaning of shahid). The shuhada here are those who testified to the truth, who stood up for it, who bore witness to it in their lives. Every person who says لا إله إلا الله and testifies to its truth becomes a kind of shahid. The prophets came with the message, and the shuhada carried it forward. They stood up for truth even when it was unpopular, even when it was dangerous.

In his farewell address, the Prophet ﷺ told the companions: "Let those who are present convey this to those who are absent." He was appointing all of them as witnesses. And Allah ﷻ calls this Ummah شُهَدَاءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ - "witnesses over humanity" in Surah Al-Baqarah (2:143).

In the next Surah we'll study after this one, we'll see a detailed account of what it means to be a shahid - someone who stands up for truth.

وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ- "And judgment will be passed among them with truth," with absolute justice.

وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ - "And they will not be wronged at all." Not in the slightest degree.

translation: And every soul will be fully compensated [for] what it did; and He is most knowing of what they do
commentary:
**وَوُفِّيَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا عَمِلَتْ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ**

وَوُفِّيَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا عَمِلَتْ - "And every soul will be fully compensated for what it did."

The verb وُفِّيَتْ comes from the root و-ف-ي, meaning to fulfill completely, to give in full measure. No deed will be overlooked. No action will go unaccounted for. Everything will be requited in full.

وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ - "And He is most knowing of what they do."

Notice the shift to يَفْعَلُونَ - the present/ongoing tense. This refers to the things people do that they might not even be conscious of. The word عَمِلَ  typically refers to deliberate work or action, but فَعَلَ can include actions done unconsciously, habitually, reflexively.

Allah ﷻ knows not just your conscious choices but your unconscious patterns, your automatic responses, the things you do without thinking. Even those will be accounted for because they reveal the state of your heart and the habits you've cultivated.

translation: And those who disbelieved will be driven to Hell in groups until, when they reach it, its gates are opened and its keepers will say, "Did there not come to you messengers from yourselves, reciting to you the verses of your Lord and warning you of the meeting of this Day of yours?" They will say, "Yes, but the word of punishment has come into effect upon the disbelievers
commentary:
**وَسِيقَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ زُمَرًا ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُوهَا فُتِحَتْ أَبْوَٰبُهَا وَقَالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَآ أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّنكُمْ يَتْلُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّكُمْ وَيُنذِرُونَكُمْ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَـٰذَا ۚ قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ وَلَـٰكِنْ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَةُ ٱلْعَذَابِ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ**

Now we witness the procession to Hellfire: وَسِيقَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ زُمَرً - "And those who disbelieved will be driven to Hell in groups."

The verb سِيقَ means to drive or herd animals, to rush them along. There's an urgency, a forceful movement. These people aren't walking calmly - they're being herded, rushed, driven forward.

زُمَرًا  - "in groups." The word زُمَر means organized groups or categories. This connects directly to the theme of the entire Surah, which is called Az-Zumar (The Groups). The disbelievers will be categorized and herded in distinct groups - perhaps by the type of sin they committed. The people of riba together, the mockers together, the oppressors together, the false gods and their worshipers together. Each group herded along toward their destination.

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُوهَا فُتِحَتْ أَبْوَٰبُهَا  - "Until when they reach it, its gates will be opened."

This is profound. Notice when the gates open - not before they arrive, but when they arrive. The doors of Hellfire remain closed as a prison should be, and they're only opened when inmates arrive. This means something crucial: as they're approaching, they can't see inside yet. But they can hear it. They can hear it roaring, screaming, raging. The sound gets louder and louder as they get closer. The terror builds with every step.

Then when they finally arrive, the gates open, and they see what was making all that noise.

وَقَالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَآ - "And its keepers will say to them" - the angels guarding the gates of Hell will ask them:

أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّنكُمْ  - "Did there not come to you messengers from among yourselves?"

Notice the emphasis: مِّنكُمْ - "from among you." They weren't alien beings. They were people like you, who spoke your language, understood your culture, lived among you.

يَتْلُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّكُمْ  - "Reciting to you the verses of your Lord," conveying the message clearly and repeatedly.

وَيُنذِرُونَكُمْ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَـٰذَا - "And warning you of the meeting of this Day of yours?"

They weren't keeping secrets. They told you exactly what would happen. They warned you about this very moment you're experiencing right now.

قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ  - "They will say: Yes, indeed."

They can't deny it. The evidence is too overwhelming. Yes, messengers came. Yes, they conveyed the message. Yes, they warned us.

وَلَـٰكِنْ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَةُ ٱلْعَذَابِ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِين - "But the decree of punishment has come into effect upon the disbelievers."

The word حَقَّتْ means to become true, to materialize, to be deserved and fulfilled. The verdict has been passed. The warning came true. The promise of punishment has manifested.

translation: [To them] it will be said, "Enter the gates of Hell to abide eternally therein, and wretched is the residence of the arrogant
commentary:
**قِيلَ ٱدْخُلُوٓا۟ أَبْوَٰبَ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ فَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ**

The command comes: قِيلَ ٱدْخُلُوٓا۟ أَبْوَٰبَ جَهَنَّمَ  - "It will be said: Enter the gates of Hell."

Notice the passive voice - "it will be said." This might be spoken by the angels, or it might be a command that simply comes from Allah ﷻ Himself, echoing through that moment.

خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا  - "To remain therein eternally."

The word خَـٰلِد comes from خُلُود, meaning perpetuity, eternity. This is permanent residence, not a temporary punishment.

فَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ  - "And wretched is the residence of the arrogant."

The word مَثْوَى means a dwelling place for long duration, generation after generation. And once again, Allah ﷻ identifies the root cause: الْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ  - the arrogant ones. It was their arrogance that prevented them from accepting truth, from submitting to Allah ﷻ, from humbling themselves before their Creator. And now their arrogance has earned them eternal humiliation.

translation: But those who feared their Lord will be driven to Paradise in groups until, when they reach it while its gates have been opened and its keepers say, "Peace be upon you; you have become pure; so enter it to abide eternally therein," [they will enter]
commentary:
**وَسِيقَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ رَبَّهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْجَنَّةِ زُمَرًا ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُوهَا وَفُتِحَتْ أَبْوَٰبُهَا وَقَالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَا سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ طِبْتُمْ فَٱدْخُلُوهَا خَـٰلِدِينَ**

Now comes the contrasting scene, deliberately parallel in structure to emphasize the difference in destination: وَسِيقَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ رَبَّهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْجَنَّةِ زُمَرًا - "And those who had taqwa of their Lord will be driven to Paradise in groups."

The same verb سِيقَ is used - but why? Why would the people of Paradise need to be "driven" or "herded"? The wisdom here is beautiful. Allah ﷻ doesn't want them lingering on the plains of Judgment, watching the punishment of others, dwelling on the horrors. He wants them rushed to safety, moved quickly to their reward. Get them to Paradise fast so they don't have to witness too much of the suffering of the Day of Judgment.

Also زُمَرًا - they too are in groups. But these are groups of honor. The people of salah together. The people of charity together. The people of dawah together. The people of patience together. The people who left haram jobs for halal income together. The people who sent salawat on the Prophet ﷺ excessively. The people who memorized Quran. The people who fed the hungry. Everyone categorized by their specializations in goodness.

We're supposed to do all good deeds, but we excel in different areas, and those excellences will be recognized and celebrated.

حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُوهَا وَفُتِحَتْ أَبْوَٰبُهَا - "Until when they reach it, and its gates have been opened."

Did you catch the difference? Look at the grammar carefully. For Hellfire it said فُتِحَتْ after they arrived - the gates opened when they got there. But for Paradise it says وَفُتِحَتْ - "and its gates had been opened." The و here with the past tense indicates the gates were already open before they arrived.

Why would the gates of Paradise be held open? There's no one trying to escape. But it's a gesture of welcome. The doors are already flung wide open, waiting for them. "Come on in! We've been expecting you! We've been waiting for you!"

This is the difference between entering a prison where the doors are locked until you arrive, versus arriving at a celebration where the doors are held open and everyone inside is waiting to welcome you.

وَقَالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَا - "And its keepers will say to them."

These angels were created for this purpose. They've been guarding these gates, maintaining Paradise, waiting for this moment when they finally get to fulfill their purpose.

سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ (salamun 'alaykum) - "Peace be upon you!"

Not just peace, but PEACE. The word سَلَامٌ in this context carries all the meanings of safety, security, wellness, wholeness, perfection. You're safe now. You made it. All the struggles are over.

طِبْتُمْ - "You were good!" or "You are excellent!" or "How wonderful you are!"

The root ط-ي-ب means goodness, purity, excellence. The angels are complimenting them, celebrating them. You were good people walking through this world. You maintained your goodness despite all the corruption around you.

فَٱدْخُلُوهَا خَـٰلِدِينَ - "So enter it to abide therein eternally."

The same word خَـٰلِدِين used for Hell is now used for Paradise. Both are eternal - but what a difference in what that eternity contains.

translation: And they will say, "Praise to Allah, who has fulfilled for us His promise and made us inherit the earth [so] we may settle in Paradise wherever we will. And excellent is the reward of [righteous] workers
commentary:
**وَقَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى صَدَقَنَا وَعْدَهُۥ وَأَوْرَثَنَا ٱلْأَرْضَ نَتَبَوَّأُ مِنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ حَيْثُ نَشَآءُ ۖ فَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ ٱلْعَـٰمِلِينَ**

The people of Paradise respond: وَقَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ - "And they will say: All praise belongs to Allah."

We say الحمد لله all the time, dozens of times a day. But imagine saying it in Paradise. Imagine saying it after Judgment Day is over, after you've been saved from Hellfire, after you've entered eternal bliss. That الحمد لله hits differently. That's the الحمد لله being described here.

ٱلَّذِى صَدَقَنَا وَعْدَهُۥ - "The One who fulfilled His promise to us."

He promised us that taqwa would lead to success. He promised us that righteousness would be rewarded. He promised us Paradise was real. And He kept every promise. صَدَقَنَا - He told us the truth.

وَأَوْرَثَنَا ٱلْأَرْضَ - "And who has granted us inheritance of the earth."

The word وَرِثَ means to inherit - to receive something that belonged to someone before you. This is a new earth, not the earth of this world. But the concept of inheritance reminds us: this goes back to our father Adam (AS). He was given an earth, then lost it through disobedience. Now his righteous descendants are finally inheriting the earth he should have had - Paradise.

نَتَبَوَّأُ مِنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ حَيْثُ نَشَآءُ - "We may settle in Paradise wherever we please."

Imagine arriving in a place and being told, "Go ahead, pick wherever you want to live." There's no competition, no property disputes, no "that's mine, not yours." Just walk around, explore, and when you find a place you love, it's yours. Then you keep walking and find another place you love - that's yours too.

This is impossible to imagine in our world where real estate is scarce and contested. But in Paradise, wherever you want to settle, whatever catches your eye, it's yours.

فَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ ٱلْعَـٰمِلِينَ - "And excellent is the reward of those who worked."

The word عَـٰمِلِين refers to those who put themselves to work, who exerted effort, who labored. This wasn't accidental. This wasn't luck. They worked for this, and now they're celebrating the fruits of that work.

translation: And you will see the angels surrounding the Throne, exalting [Allah] with praise of their Lord. And it will be judged between them in truth, and it will be said, "[All] praise to Allah, Lord of the worlds
commentary:
**وَتَرَى ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ حَآفِّينَ مِنْ حَوْلِ ٱلْعَرْشِ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ ۖ وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ وَقِيلَ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ**

The Surah concludes with a magnificent vision: وَتَرَى ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ حَآفِّينَ مِنْ حَوْلِ ٱلْعَرْشِ - "And you will see the angels surrounding the Throne on all sides."

Some say this address is specifically to the Prophet ﷺ because ordinary believers won't see the angels. But the vision being described is meant for all of us to contemplate. The angels, in rows upon rows, surrounding the Throne of Allah ﷻ.

يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ - "Glorifying their Lord with praise."

After everything that's happened - the resurrection, the judgment, the sorting into Paradise and Hell - the angels continue their perpetual task: declaring Allah's ﷻ perfection and praising Him.

وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ - "And judgment has been passed among them with truth."

Even among the angels, there are matters to be resolved. We learned earlier in the Surah that the Prophet ﷺ was told, "I don't know about the disputes of the highest angels." Even they have differences, and even those differences are resolved on the Day of Judgment with perfect justice.

وَقِيلَ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ - "And it will be said: All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all the worlds."

The Surah ends where Surah Al-Fatihah begins. Everywhere you turn in Paradise, from the highest levels, from the Throne itself, the only words that can be heard are the words we say at the beginning of every prayer: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ.

This is meant to transform how we recite Al-Fatihah. When you say الحمد لله رب العالمين, you should remember this scene. You should imagine the angels surrounding the Throne. You should imagine everyone having entered Paradise. You should imagine all matters resolved with perfect justice. And from the highest palace of Paradise, from above the Throne itself, this declaration echoes through all of creation: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ.

This phrase that we say casually dozens of times a day is actually a window into the eternal reality that awaits. It gives us an otherworldly, transcendent view of these beautiful words that we're commanded to say in every unit of every prayer.

With this vision of ultimate victory, of perfect justice, of eternal praise, Surah Az-Zumar concludes. The Surah began with the command to worship Allah ﷻ alone with sincere devotion, and it ends with all of creation - angels and humans alike - united in praising Him, each in their proper place, having received perfect justice. The arrogant have been humbled, the humble have been exalted, and all praise returns to where it eternally belongs: to Allah ﷻ, Lord of all the worlds.
